title: Materialize generic LOB locator cells with thin driver reads
description:
## Current gap

`rust-oracledb` already exposes public LOB locator reads through `Connection::read_lob` / blocking `read_lob`, and oraclemcp already has capped LOB-reading logic for explicit `oracle_read_clob` style workflows. Generic query serialization still emits placeholders for `QueryValue::Lob` in `crates/oraclemcp-db/src/connection.rs`:

`<unsupported LOB locator size=...>`

That makes ordinary `SELECT clob_col FROM ...` or PL/SQL output returning LOB locators less useful to agents even though the driver can read the locator.

## Required change

Materialize LOB locator cells during generic query/result conversion when the driver returns `QueryValue::Lob`, using the driver's public locator-read API and the existing oraclemcp caps/serialization policy.

## Safety requirements

- Preserve hard caps for text characters and binary bytes.
- Preserve type-specific output: CLOB/NCLOB as capped text, BLOB/BFILE as capped binary/base64 or existing binary representation, with truncation metadata.
- Do not read unbounded LOBs by default.
- Respect call timeout/cancellation handling where available.
- Keep errors structured: unsupported LOB subtype or failed locator read should be explicit, not silently replaced with null.

## Acceptance criteria

- Generic query rows no longer emit unsupported placeholders for supported LOB locators.
- Existing explicit LOB tools keep working.
- Unit tests cover CLOB, BLOB, null LOB, truncation, and locator-read failure paths.
- Live 23ai/FREE tests cover selecting CLOB/BLOB values through `oracle_query` with caps.
- Performance is bounded: tests or benchmarks prove a large LOB cannot force unbounded allocation.
close_reason: Implemented bounded generic LOB locator materialization for CLOB/BLOB/BFILE, wired oracle_query serialization caps through the backend, added unit tests and bound live 23ai coverage; gates, full workspace tests, cargo deny, and single-bead audit passed.

title: 0.6.0 Interactive & Always-On — release train 0.6.0→0.6.1→0.6.2
description:
# Epic: oraclemcp 0.6.0 "Interactive & Always-On" (release TRAIN 0.6.0 → 0.6.1 → 0.6.2)

Source of truth at beading time: PLAN_0_6_0_INTERACTIVE_ALWAYS_ON.md v3.18 (2787 lines). These beads
elaborate that plan and are intended to be SELF-CONTAINED — you should not need to reopen the markdown.

## What we are building (TL;DR)
oraclemcp becomes ONE guarded always-on service + ONE versioned schema-first protocol + MANY front-ends,
with a first-class per-principal HTTP **LaneRuntime** as the internal security & concurrency boundary.
Two access modes, decoupled:
- **stdio — UNCHANGED.** Each agent spawns its own process → own connection/level/DB. Single-principal
  per process, already correct. DO NOT TOUCH IT (non-goal N10).
- **http — UPGRADED** to true per-lane concurrency. One persistent service; each caller resolves to a
  **lane** bound to a **verified Subject** (never caller-supplied), with its own DB/profile, operating
  level, single-use grant store, request budget, audit context, cancellation. Many agents + a human →
  many databases at once, isolated. The **web dashboard** is the human face.

## The release train (decision D1 — one plan, beaded together, built continuously, NO deferral)
- **0.6.0 — Foundation (safety core + usable product):** driver 0.5.1 validation (WP-A), #4/#3/#2
  (WP-B/C/R), **WP-N lane foundation**, WP-P protocol, WP-S service, WP-E/F installer/distribution,
  WP-G core + `om doctor`, WP-H release. Dashboard = the READ-ONLY control plane (Overview/Sessions/
  Audit/Doctor; 2D first; skinnable seam in place).
- **0.6.1 — Interactive dashboard:** full views + the governed Workbench (Safe SQL, governed edit loop,
  live compile, schema browser, global search) + plsql-intelligence wiring + version history.
- **0.6.2 — Advanced/wow:** Change-Review board ("PR for PL/SQL") + the 3D Orrery skin + migration export.
Train membership is carried as a label (train:0.6.0 / train:0.6.1 / train:0.6.2) on every bead.

## Throughline (NON-NEGOTIABLE safety invariant — AGENTS.md "do not weaken")
Every face goes through the SAME fail-closed core: classifier → per-lane ceiling → preview/confirm →
rollback-by-default → audit. Identity is a verified Subject (dashboard login / OAuth sub / mTLS fp /
local pairing / OS user) — NEVER self-supplied in tool args. Loopback hub; dashboard requires local
pairing/auth even on loopback; no foreign-config mutation; no raw PTY/free-SQL terminal; secrets are
external refs; uncertain DB outcomes poison/quarantine the connection. Ladder: READ_ONLY < READ_WRITE
< DDL < ADMIN. Never bypass the classifier, never exceed a profile max_level, never make a protected
profile writable, never auto-commit DML, never admit an unproven statement.

## Ground truth that shapes the work (verified — §1, §4-GT)
- Repo at 0.4.1; `oracledb = "=0.5.1"` ALREADY pinned (WP-A = validation, NOT a version bump).
- The fail-closed GUARD ALREADY SHIPS in crates/oraclemcp-guard (classifier 104KB, OperatingLevel
  ladder + DangerLevel, 3-valued purity.rs with a SideEffectOracle port, stepup.rs confirm-token,
  token.rs AllowOnceStore single-use grants). **WP-N builds the per-lane LANE layer OVER the existing
  guard — it does NOT re-implement the classifier.**
- `#4` timeout_ms is already threaded through collect_all_rows/fetch_cursor/read_lob (WP-B = verify +
  shared-layer default + bounded ROLLBACK, not re-plumb).
- plsql-intelligence v0.7.0, ~21 PURE engine crates (no tokio/net) → bake in via (1) the SideEffectOracle
  purity port [safety core, 0.6.0-eligible] and (2) the Workbench IDE [0.6.1].
- Runtime: asupersync 0.3.4 (pinned nightly-2026-05-11 for try_trait_v2). Appendix A of the plan is the
  SOURCE-VERIFIED asupersync API reference every WP-N bead must cite by id; Appendix B is the per-WP
  acceptance-test spec (incl. B.13 edge cases). Pure-Rust, #![forbid(unsafe_code)], no tokio/axum/hyper
  (boundary-linted).

## Sub-epics (one per work package, matching plan §10)
WP-A driver validation · WP-B timeout/poison (#4) · WP-C non-lossy serialization (#3) · WP-R routine
exec (#2) · WP-N per-principal LaneRuntime (THE FOUNDATION) · WP-P operator protocol · WP-S persistent
service · WP-W web dashboard · WP-E installer · WP-F distribution · WP-G hardening/docs · WP-H release.

## Phase-0 release-blockers for 0.6.0 (gate the whole train)
CX-I6 capacity spike (justify 16/8/64 lane caps; lane ≈ 2 OS threads), CX-I7 panic-isolation prototype
(catch_unwind/drop/quarantine/audit), CX-C1 durable write-ahead idempotency (no double-execute across
restart). All under WP-N.

## How to work these beads
Front-load WP-N (N8 → N0a → N0 → …) + the Phase-0 spikes. Every implementation bead names its
applicable D15 cheap-change rule in its DoD and carries an Appendix-B test companion. Use `br ready`
to find unblocked work; `bv --robot-next` to pick. Do NOT weaken the safety invariant.

Tests = Appendix B.1.

title: TEST-HARNESS — structured JSON-line logging + e2e runner scripts (the test standard)
description: The test and logging STANDARD that EVERY test bead inherits (the comprehensive-tests + detailed-logging requirement). (1) UNIT: every implementation bead lands with its unit tests green + its named Appendix-B test. (2) E2E SCRIPTS: runnable scripts under scripts/e2e/ (one per scenario), each accepting --log to emit STRUCTURED JSON-LINE logs to stderr (fields: event, phase one-of setup/act/assert/teardown, ts, duration_ms, lane, subject, sid, profile, level, grant, outcome); a top-level orchestrator runs them in order and aggregates a pass/fail summary. (3) DETERMINISM + REPLAY: lab/concurrency tests use cx.now()/cx.random_u64() (no wall-clock/ambient-rand); on failure emit a CRASHPACK + SEED so the bad run replays (asupersync lab, Appendix A.13). (4) PROD-SAFETY: live/e2e tests env-gated (ORACLEMCP_TEST_* / ORACLEMCP_LIVE_XE=1), never touch a non-test DB; a production DSN/URL blocklist. (5) NO-MOCKS for Score-at-least-8 paths (cross-lane isolation, commit/rollback, grant consumption, audit-append, credential issuance) — real Oracle 23ai / real files; a trait-double is allowed only for deterministic lab interleavings. (6) COVERAGE accounting for conformance (MUST coverage at least 0.95; XFAIL not SKIP; fixture provenance generator+version+cmd). DoD: the JSON-line logger + the scripts/e2e orchestrator + the crashpack/seed emitter exist; every test bead uses them. D15 rule 6 (tests pin contracts). No deps.
close_reason: Added scripts/e2e JSON-line harness, orchestrator, crashpack/seed failure artifacts, live safety gates, conformance coverage/provenance docs, and Rust contract tests; verified dry-run and offline scenarios.

title: DL-9 — concurrency-audit CI lint (The Fourth Instance)
description: A concurrency-audit CI lint enforcing the asupersync audit greps as GATES: block_on outside the one sanctioned lane-bridge module = fail; tokio::spawn or std::sync::Mutex in core = fail; unbounded / mpsc::unbounded channel = fail (every queue bounded); a lane module importing another lane Sender = fail (DL-3); loops missing cx.checkpoint() in lane/handler modules = warn+review; ObligationLeak/FuturelockViolation/RegionCloseTimeout in cargo test output = fail. DoD: lint runs each PR; joins HCI.
close_reason: Implemented the DL-9 concurrency-audit gate: added scripts/oraclemcp_concurrency_lint.sh with production scans for unsanctioned block_on, tokio::spawn, std::sync::Mutex in core, unbounded queues, lane command sender leaks, loop checkpoint review warnings, and cargo-test marker failures for ObligationLeak/FuturelockViolation/RegionCloseTimeout; wired it into PR CI, release gates, and release_preflight; converted remaining production core std::sync::Mutex stores to parking_lot and removed stale poisoned-lock errors; marked existing intentional block_on bridge sites. Verified fmt, clippy, workspace all-target tests with marker scan, cargo deny, boundary, driver seam, sensitive data lint, release_preflight, and git diff --check.

title: ERG-10 — agent-ergonomics drift-guard CI
description: A CI drift-guard for the om ergonomics contract: golden tests pinning om --help footers + capabilities --json schema + the exit-code dict + the MCP-CLI-dashboard parity matrix. DoD: drift-guard runs each PR; joins HCI. Depends ERG (cross).
close_reason: Added PR CI ergonomics drift guard for help footer, capabilities schema, exit-code dictionary, and MCP/CLI/dashboard parity matrix.

title: WP-B — Timeout hardening + poison/quarantine (#4)
description: WP-B sub-epic. timeout_ms already threaded through collect_all_rows/fetch_cursor/read_lob (§4-GT.1) — VERIFY + shared-layer default + total wall-clock request budget + bounded ROLLBACK + B1c poison/quarantine. Budget algebra = asupersync Budget.meet (A.5): effective Budget = root.meet(ceiling).meet(deadline) replaces #4's three timers. Poison: any timeout/network-error/cancel-deadline/rollback-failure/CLOSE-WAIT/unknown-COMMIT → conn poisoned, dropped, never pooled, grants revoked, audit outcome class (rolled_back|discarded_uncommitted|commit_in_doubt|unknown_discarded). oracledb signals: connection_disposition()→Reusable|Dead, is_connection_lost(), CallTimeout/ConnectionClosed. Tests = Appendix B.2. Closes #4.
close_reason: WP-B complete: B1 request budget/default timeout/rollback cleanup, B1b bounded collect_all_rows continuation fetches, B1c poison/quarantine, B2 dirty-discard/no poisoned pool reuse, B2.7 acceptance suite with live Oracle fetch and load-soak proof, B4 issue #4 proof comment, and B3 upstream EXPIRE_TIME tracking are all closed.

title: B1 — shared-layer default timeout + total request budget + bounded ROLLBACK
description: Add a shared-layer DEFAULT call timeout (so callers like plsql-mcp using oraclemcp-db directly stop getting None) + a TOTAL wall-clock request budget + a BOUNDED ROLLBACK (and bounded COMMIT for in-doubt). Implement via asupersync Budget.meet (Appendix A.5): effective Budget = service-root.meet(profile-ceiling).meet(per-request-deadline), carrying deadline+poll-quota+cost-quota, propagated to children (tighter, never INFINITE). The driver per-op deadline is Query::timeout/Execute::timeout (one deadline spanning continuation fetches + LOB reads). doctor warns when a timeout is 0/unset. Why: #4 is a hang/timeout bug; the budget algebra unifies the three knobs #4 treated separately. DoD: budget threaded; doctor warn; B.2 budget_meet_replaces_three_timers. Edits connection.rs.
close_reason: Implemented shared 30s DB default timeout, dispatcher RequestBudget meet plumbing, doctor timeout warning, bounded rollback adapter cleanup, docs, API baseline, and tests/gates.

title: B1b — per-batch fetch-loop timeout (single wrap at collect_all_rows)
description: Bound the fetch loop per-batch with ONE wrap at collect_all_rows (timeout_ms is already threaded there per §4-GT.1 — verify + enforce). A slow continuation fetch → typed CallTimeout, never an unbounded loop. Why: an unbounded fetch loop is a hang vector (#4). DoD: B.2 fetch_loop_is_bounded_per_batch (FakeBackend + live).
close_reason: Bound collect_all_rows parent-result continuation fetches with per-batch call timeout, cancel/drain recovery, cursor cleanup, fake timeout proof, and live-gated continuation proof; closeout gates pass.

title: B1c — poison/quarantine contract (uncertain outcome → never reuse)
description: THE safety contract for uncertain DB outcomes. Any timeout / network error / cancellation-deadline / rollback-failure / CLOSE-WAIT / unknown-COMMIT marks the lane connection POISONED → dropped, never pooled, OPEN GRANTS REVOKED, and the audit records the outcome class: rolled_back | discarded_uncommitted | commit_in_doubt | unknown_discarded. Use the driver signals: error.connection_disposition() → Reusable|Dead, is_connection_lost(), CallTimeout vs ConnectionClosed (drain-fail = lost). Run rollback + the audit append as masked/commit_section finalizers (Appendix A.9) so cleanup completes deterministically under shutdown/cancel. Why: prevents a half-known session from leaking state into the next caller (invariant N5/B1c). DoD: B.2 poisoned_conn_is_never_reused + commit_in_doubt_marks_quarantine. Ties to WP-N N5 + B1c lane teardown.
close_reason: Implemented DB session quarantine outcomes, direct dispatcher commit-in-doubt quarantine, lease discard-on-uncertain cleanup, lane panic unknown-discard audit, API baseline/docs, and full gates.

title: B2 — verify dirty-discard + no poisoned reuse in the pool
description: Verify a cancelled/failed pooled call is DISCARDED (dirty), never returned idle for reuse; poisoned conns never re-enter the pool. Why: defense-in-depth for B1c at the pool boundary. DoD: B.2 dirty_discard_no_pool_return (db chaos).
close_reason: Added dirty_discard_no_pool_return pool proof: dirty check-in is not returned to idle, increments discarded, frees capacity only for a fresh replacement slot; full gates pass.

title: B3 — track upstream rust-oracledb #14 (EXPIRE_TIME parsed-not-applied)
description: Track upstream rust-oracledb #14 (EXPIRE_TIME parsed but not applied as TCP keepalive). Independent of the other B work. DoD: a tracking note + a test asserting current behavior (parsed into Description.expire_time) so a future upstream fix is detected. Backlog-ish; do not block #4 close on it.
close_reason: Tracked rust-oracledb#14 current state (open; v0.5.1 honors transport connect timeout but EXPIRE_TIME keepalive remains upstream-owned), added operations note, and added connection::driver_seam::upstream_expire_time_gap_is_parse_visible asserting EXPIRE_TIME parses into Description::expire_time. Checks: fmt, driver_seam tests, clippy -p oraclemcp-db, boundary lint, api lock, sensitive-data lint, diff check.

title: B4 — close issue #4 after timeout/poison validated
description: Close GitHub issue #4 (hang/timeout) ONLY after B1/B1b/B1c/B2 land and live-XE soak passes. DoD: #4 closed with proof (soak output); upgrade note if behavior changed.
close_reason: GitHub issue #4 was already closed; added WP-B closeout proof at https://github.com/MuhDur/oraclemcp/issues/4#issuecomment-4846069119. Evidence: B1/B1b/B1c/B2/B2.7 closed; live fetch-loop timeout passed; live load_soak passed 1600 ops/8 clients balanced p50=975us p95=3125us p99=4265us; local gates green.

title: Tests B.2 — WP-B timeout/poison acceptance suite
description: Appendix B.2. budget_meet_replaces_three_timers (U/P | cancel_correctness); fetch_loop_is_bounded_per_batch (P | oracledb_contract FakeBackend + live_oracle); poisoned_conn_is_never_reused (L/E | db chaos + load_soak; connection_disposition()==Dead → dropped/grants-revoked/audit-class); commit_in_doubt_marks_quarantine (L | cancel_correctness; drain-fail → ConnectionClosed → quarantine); dirty_discard_no_pool_return (U/E | db chaos). DoD: all green; #4 closeable. Depends B1,B1b,B1c,B2.
close_reason: WP-B B.2 acceptance suite green: RequestBudget meet/cleanup tests, fetch_loop_is_bounded_per_batch fake test, live fetch-loop timeout against local Oracle FREE, pool dirty-discard/lease quarantine tests, cancel_correctness, dispatcher commit-in-doubt quarantine, load_soak accounting, and opt-in live load_soak passed (1600 ops/8 clients balanced; p50=975us p95=3125us p99=4265us).

title: WP-C — Non-lossy serialization (#3)
description: WP-C sub-epic. OracleCell.structured carrier; type ARRAY/JSON(OsonValue)/VECTOR(Dense|Sparse)/TSTZ(offset_minutes); object identity (schema,type_name)+typed-unsupported EVERYWHERE (driver has NO Opaque variant — oraclemcp owns the marker; nested object → driver UnsupportedFeature); capped non-default catalog mode; publish OracleCell.structured JSON schema + golden fixtures (C4b); serialization contract-version tag (C5) consumed by dashboard/schema caches. NUMBER stays lossless string by default. Tests = Appendix B.3 + B.13#2 boundary values. Closes #3 + upgrade note.
close_reason: All WP-C child beads are closed: structured OracleCell carrier, typed ARRAY/JSON/VECTOR/TSTZ serialization, typed unsupported/object identity handling, deep-decode catalog mode, schema/goldens, contract-version tagging, acceptance tests, and issue #3 closeout/upgrade note are complete.

title: C0 — OracleCell.structured carrier (+ contract)
description: Add a structured carrier to OracleCell so non-scalar Oracle values survive serialization without lossy flattening. Background §4-GT.1/#3: OracleCell has no structured carrier today; the contract suite (oracledb_contract.rs::contract_type_*) + type_fidelity.rs already pin number-lossless-string / NLS-invariance / unsupported-marker. Why: foundation for C1/C2. DoD: carrier type + a contract test; NUMBER stays lossless string by default (numbers_as_float opt-in only). Edits connection.rs (sequence with C1-C3). Test: B.3.
close_reason: Added OracleCell structured JSON carrier and constructor, serialized structured payloads verbatim ahead of scalar text, and pinned the public contract with unit + oracledb_contract tests while preserving NUMBER lossless-string behavior. Refreshed oraclemcp-db API lock. Verified fmt, clippy, workspace tests, deny, boundary, driver seam, sensitive-data, honesty, api-lock, release_preflight.

title: C1 — typed ARRAY/JSON/VECTOR/TSTZ serialization
description: Serialize the 0.5.1 structured QueryValue variants: Array(Vec<Option<QueryValue>>), Json(OsonValue), Vector(Dense|Sparse × Float32/Float64/Int8/Binary), TimestampTz{offset_minutes}. Invertive MR: serialize→parse→equal. Why: non-lossy #3. DoD: B.3 structured_carrier_round_trips_array_json_vector_tstz (MR + golden + schema) + B.13#2 boundary values (empty/nested array, sparse zero-index, OSON depth-cap±1). Coordinates with A2 (TSTZ).
close_reason: Implemented typed structured serialization for QueryValue Array, Json(OSON), and Vector values in the existing oracledb adapter, using OracleCell::structured instead of lossy Debug/text rendering. The carrier preserves SQL NULL vs OSON null, NUMBER text, raw bytes, DateTime/Interval scalars, nested TSTZ offset_minutes, dense/sparse vectors, binary vectors, and sparse zero indices. Added adapter unit tests plus type_fidelity and shared oracledb_contract coverage for array/json/vector/tstz round-trip JSON shape. Verified fmt, clippy, workspace tests, concurrency lint, deny, boundary, driver seam, sensitive-data, honesty, api-lock, and release_preflight.

title: C2 — object identity + typed-unsupported EVERYWHERE
description: For UDT/object values (driver ObjectValue{schema,type_name,packed_data}) emit (schema,type_name) + a typed-unsupported MARKER; nested object/collection attribute → explicit driver UnsupportedFeature, NEVER a silent flatten. The driver has NO Opaque variant — oraclemcp OWNS the unsupported marker. BFILE = Lob{ora_type_num=114}. Why: honest, never-silent #3. DoD: B.3 object_identity_marker_never_silent (extends existing contract_type_unsupported_*) + B.13#2.
close_reason: Implemented typed unsupported Oracle object markers with schema/type identity and nested object decode coverage; verified gates.

title: C3 — capped non-default catalog (deep-decode) mode
description: A capped, non-default mode for deeper catalog/object decode (row/cell/byte/depth caps) behind an opt-in flag; default stays the safe capped shape. Why: power users may want more without making the default heavy/unsafe. DoD: caps enforced; default unchanged; B.3 + B.13#2 caps±1.
close_reason: Implemented capped structured deep decode mode with query opt-in, row/cell/byte/depth caps, schema docs, and acceptance tests.

title: C4 — close issue #3 + upgrade note
description: Close #3 after C0-C3/C4b land. Ships an UPGRADE NOTE (serialized-shape change is documented, N7 — no silent change). DoD: #3 closed; CHANGELOG upgrade note (coordinate G2).
close_reason: Closed GitHub issue #3 after the structured OracleCell serialization contract, schema/goldens, acceptance tests, and CHANGELOG upgrade note were pushed to main.

description: Publish the OracleCell.structured JSON schema + golden fixtures (ARRAY/JSON/VECTOR/TSTZ/object/unsupported) with PROVENANCE (generator+version+cmd). Why: agents/SPA consume a stable typed shape; goldens are the refactor safety net (D15 rule 6). DoD: schema file + goldens reblessable via UPDATE_GOLDENS; B.3 (G) + conformance coverage. Feeds A7b.

description: Tag the serialized shape with a contract version consumed by the dashboard/schema caches (W7 cache key includes it) so a shape bump invalidates caches deterministically. Why: schema-first additive evolution (D15 rule 4). DoD: tag present + a test that W7 cache keys on it; B.3 serialization_contract_version_present_and_consumed.
close_reason: Added structured OracleCell contract version tag, schema tag, and metadata cache key coverage; verified full gates.

title: Tests B.3 + B.13#2 — WP-C serialization acceptance + boundary values
description: Appendix B.3 + B.13#2. number_is_lossless_string / nls_invariance (MR/G | type_fidelity, exist — equivalence MR under NLS changes); structured_carrier_round_trips_array_json_vector_tstz (MR/G/C | type_fidelity + oracledb_contract; invertive MR + goldens + schema); object_identity_marker_never_silent (U/G | contract_type_unsupported_*; nested → UnsupportedFeature, never silent); serialization_contract_version_present_and_consumed (C | mcp_conformance + W7 cache). B.13#2 boundary values: NULL every type; empty/nested Array; Vector::Sparse zero-index; Json depth-cap and cap+1; CLOB byte-cap and cap+1; BLOB base64 len%3 in {0,1,2}; NUMBER 38-digit + negative scale; BinaryDouble +Inf/NaN/-0.0; IntervalDS/YM; TimestampTz offset 0/+-14:00/9-digit/DST. DoD: all green; #3 closeable. Depends C1,C2,C4b,C5.
close_reason: Covered WP-C serialization acceptance and boundary catalog in type_fidelity tests; full gates pass.

title: WP-R — Routine execution (#2)
description: WP-R sub-epic. Non-Deserialize OracleRoutineArg (wraps driver BindValue::{Output,ReturnOutput,ObjectOutput}; driver confirms NO serde bound, no IN-OUT-specific variant — Output covers both; retrieve via ExecuteOutcome::out_binds()). Adapter-internal call_routine, deterministic positional order + COMMIT caveat. Grep-lint: call_routine absent from oraclemcp AND oraclemcp-core public surface (no agent tool, N9). Tests = Appendix B.4. Closes #2.
close_reason: WP-R complete: R1-R4 and B.4 acceptance closed; GitHub issue #2 closed with live proof.

title: R1 — non-Deserialize OracleRoutineArg
description: Add OracleRoutineArg wrapping the driver's BindValue::{Output,ReturnOutput,ObjectOutput} (driver confirms NO serde/Deserialize bound on the bind path; there is NO separate IN-OUT variant — Output covers both). It MUST NOT impl Deserialize (so it can never be populated from an agent tool arg). Why: #2 routine OUT/IN-OUT support without exposing an agent-facing routine tool. DoD: B.4 routine_arg_is_not_deserialize (trybuild compile-fail under crates/oraclemcp-db/tests/ui/). Edits connection.rs.
close_reason: Added non-Deserialize OracleRoutineArg wrapper and trybuild gate

title: R2 — adapter-internal call_routine (deterministic order, COMMIT caveat)
description: Adapter-internal call_routine that binds positional OUT/IN-OUT args, executes, and retrieves OUT values via ExecuteOutcome::out_binds() in DECLARED positional order. Document the COMMIT caveat (a routine may commit internally). adapter-internal ONLY — no agent tool. DoD: B.4 call_routine_out_bind_order_deterministic (FakeBackend + live).
close_reason: Added adapter-internal call_routine with deterministic OUT bind ordering

title: R3 — grep-lint: call_routine absent from agent surface
description: A grep-lint (honesty_grep-style) asserting call_routine is ABSENT from the oraclemcp AND oraclemcp-core public surfaces (it is adapter-internal in oraclemcp-db only). Why: enforce 'no agent-facing arbitrary routine tool' (non-goal N9). DoD: B.4 call_routine_absent_from_agent_surface; lint joins the gate set.
close_reason: Added oraclemcp_agent_surface_lint gate and cargo-test mirror proving call_routine stays out of oraclemcp/oraclemcp-core agent-facing surfaces; wired it into CI boundary and release preflight. Verified fmt, clippy, workspace tests, cargo deny, release preflight, boundary, driver-seam, honesty, sensitive-data, concurrency, and focused agent-surface checks.

title: R4 — close issue #2
description: Close #2 (routine OUT/IN-OUT) after R1-R3 land + live verify. DoD: #2 closed with proof.
close_reason: Closed GitHub issue #2 after adding mixed input/output routine adapter support and live Oracle proof.

title: Tests B.4 — WP-R routine acceptance suite
description: Appendix B.4. routine_arg_is_not_deserialize (U compile-fail | new oraclemcp-db/tests/ui/, trybuild; OracleRoutineArg wraps BindValue::{Output,ReturnOutput,ObjectOutput}, no Deserialize); call_routine_out_bind_order_deterministic (U/E | oracledb_contract FakeBackend + live_oracle; ExecuteOutcome::out_binds() positional order); call_routine_absent_from_agent_surface (C grep-lint | honesty_grep; absent from oraclemcp + oraclemcp-core). DoD: all green; #2 closeable. Depends R1,R2,R3.
close_reason: B.4 acceptance suite is green: routine_arg_is_not_deserialize trybuild passed in cargo test --workspace, contract_call_routine_returns_ordered_out_binds passed in oracledb_contract, live_contract_call_routine_out_bind_order_deterministic passed against local Oracle Free with live-xe, and call_routine_absent_from_agent_surface passed via honesty_grep plus oraclemcp_agent_surface_lint.

title: WP-N — Per-principal HTTP LaneRuntime (THE FOUNDATION)
description: WP-N sub-epic — THE FOUNDATION; highest priority + hardest review. A per-lane LANE layer OVER the existing guard. Lane = OS thread owning {asupersync current-thread runtime+reactor, !Send Oracle conn, lease, level, single-use grants, request budget, audit ctx, cancel} + a Send control mailbox; registry holds only Send LaneHandles (mailbox+generation:u64+cancel+join+status), NEVER the conn. Transport = MT asupersync runtime; conn ops marshaled to the owning lane thread. Verified against asupersync 0.3.4 (Appendix A): channels A.3, cancel A.4, supervision/AppSpec A.7, name-leases A.8, combinators A.9 (bulkhead per-DB ceiling — NO asupersync Pool exists), Send/!Send seam A.10. Carries N9 (set in stone), §4-R safety (N-S1..N-S7), missing foundations (FN0-FN3), C-arch (C-1..C-6), Phase-0 release-blockers (CX-I6/I7/C1). Tests = Appendix B.5 + N9 + B.13#1/#3. stdio untouched (N10).
close_reason: Completed the WP-N foundation scope: all per-principal LaneRuntime child beads are closed, including lane ownership, per-lane connection/profile/level/grants, lifecycle/capacity/fairness, audit identity/evidence, security hardening, capability narrowing, panic/cancel/idempotency, and B.13/N9 contract coverage.

title: N8 — interim guard: 2nd concurrent principal fails closed (pre-lane)
description: INTERIM SAFETY (do first). Until N0a-N3 land, the http server must run single-principal OR FAIL CLOSED on a second concurrent principal — never silently share level/DB/grants. Background §1.1: today OracleDispatcher is one AsyncMutex<DispatcherState> with one conn/profile/level shared via Arc across HTTP threads, and confirm-tokens are HMAC over (profile,level,sql) with NO caller identity → cross-redeemable. That is a latent multi-client hole masked only by single-principal use. DoD: a 2nd concurrent principal is rejected (typed) or single-principal is enforced; B.5 I1 / N9-I1. Closes the latent hole now; superseded by N0a-N3.
close_reason: Implemented pre-lane HTTP single-principal fail-closed guard: OAuth principals derive redacted stable keys from validated claims, served HTTP config enables SinglePrincipalGuard, and second concurrent OAuth subject receives typed HTTP 409 without token/subject leakage. Verification: cargo fmt --all -- --check; cargo test -p oraclemcp --test e2e_http_oauth -- --nocapture; cargo test -p oraclemcp-core http::tests:: --lib; cargo test -p oraclemcp http_cli_oauth_builds_enforced_transport_config; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace; cargo deny check; driver/honesty/boundary/api-lock scripts; git diff --check.

title: N5 — lane lifecycle + MCP Streamable-HTTP semantics
description: Distinguish: RESPONSE-STREAM DISCONNECT (does NOT cancel — the call continues to completion / explicit CancelledNotification / budget expiry), EXPLICIT cancellation, HTTP DELETE MCP-Session-Id (terminate the lane: rollback if dirty, revoke grants, release/discard conn — as a MESSAGE to the owning lane thread, C-3), idle TTL, service shutdown (bounded drain). Dropped SSE can reconnect/resume or get a typed expired result. Failed/cancelled/uncertain → quarantine (B1c), never reuse. Record the structured CancelReason/CancelKind (Appendix A.4) in the audit chain (User=DELETE/disconnect, Timeout=B1, Shutdown=drain). DoD: N9-E1..E5 + N9-J (MCP compliance). Depends N1, N4.
notes: N5 implementation complete. Streamable HTTP DELETE invalidates the session, clears buffered replay events, invokes the stateful lifecycle hook, and terminates the lane via an owning-lane Close mailbox command. Listener shutdown drains request workers and then closes all stateful lanes with server_shutdown; runtime drop closes with runtime_drop; lifecycle close releases N4 capacity permits. OracleDispatcher cleanup drops elevation, increments grant generation, clears execute grants/compat grants/patch previews, resets the read-only backstop, rolls back the primary session, and discards the connection by lane exit. Lifecycle audit is structured: audit schema v2 includes hash-covered cancel.kind/cancel.reason, lane_lifecycle records use SQL preview LANE_CLOSE, and close reasons map to User/session_delete, Timeout/idle_timeout, Shutdown/server_shutdown, or Shutdown/runtime_drop. GET replay detects cursors older than the retained per-session ring and returns typed 410 stream_cursor_expired. C-6 proof is implemented: an initialized idle lane parked between mailbox commands wakes and closes from another OS thread; docs record the split between idle mailbox wake and in-flight Oracle call timeout/driver break/socket close plus quarantine/discard. C-1 is implemented: stateful HTTP idle TTL reaping updates last_seen on activity, removes expired replay buffers, and sends Timeout close messages through the lifecycle hook without touching the Oracle connection. Verified by cargo fmt --all -- --check, focused lifecycle/replay/audit/wake/reaper/config tests, cargo test -p oraclemcp-core --lib, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test --workspace --quiet, cargo deny check, scripts/oraclemcp_api_lock.sh, scripts/oraclemcp_boundary_lint.sh, scripts/sensitive_data_lint.sh, and git diff --check.
close_reason: Implemented lane lifecycle and Streamable HTTP semantics: DELETE/session shutdown/runtime-drop/idle-timeout lane termination via owning-lane messages, structured hash-covered lifecycle cancel audit metadata, typed replay cursor expiry, cross-thread idle wake proof, and idle TTL reaping. Verified with focused tests plus fmt, clippy, workspace tests, deny, API lock, boundary lint, sensitive-data lint, and diff check.

title: N6 — stop serializing reads on a global lock (read-worker lanes)
description: Stateless reads run CONCURRENTLY via bounded read-worker lanes (each its own reactor+conn) / per-reactor shards — NOT one shared cross-reactor OraclePool (reactor-affine, CX-1). A read lane is per (Subject, profile, DB) — never a cross-principal pool (CX-C4). Stateful work serializes only within its own lane. DoD: N9-C1 (readers no head-of-line). Depends N0a runtime.
close_reason: Routed served stateless generated metadata reads through bounded per-principal/profile read-worker lanes with profile-switch invalidation; HTTP startup no longer opens the unused shared stateless pool while stdio/direct pool behavior remains intact.

title: N7 — per-Subject audit identity + DB evidence
description: Every action records its VERIFIED Subject (no generic HumanOperator); NO caller-supplied identity (agent_identity/operator_name/label) can change it. Live DB records also capture the redacted DB fingerprint (db_unique_name/service/instance), Oracle username/proxy user, and session evidence (sid, serial#, client_identifier, module, action) when visible; absence → db_evidence_unavailable, not a failure. Depends on FN2 (Subject-aware audit rebuild). DoD: N9-G1..G4 + the headline negative test (a fake agent_identity cannot change the audit subject). Depends N0.
close_reason: Threaded audit records to server-derived subjects, attached best-effort DB evidence under audit schema 3, rejected caller-supplied identity spoofing, and verified full gates.

title: FN2 — Subject-aware audit rebuild (+DB evidence, schema bump)
description: FOUNDATION (before N7). Extend AuditRecord with a structured Subject{kind,stable_id,authn_method,client_id,thumbprint} + DB-evidence columns; preserve/upgrade verify; bump the audit schema_version (additive/format-versioned, never rewrite — DOC-5). Background §4-R: today audit is a free-form agent_identity:String + a single global chain (also a cross-lane p99 serialization point). The audit-chain writer must be transaction-style (build entry in a local, append-and-swap) so a panic mid-append can't corrupt the chain (DL-5). DoD: structured Subject in records; verify still passes across the bump; concurrent appends → valid chain (N9-G2). Prereq for N7/N-S2/W5/G9.
close_reason: Added audit schema_version=2 with structured AuditSubject and optional DbEvidence, kept signed v1 verification compatible, hash-covered v2 subject/evidence fields, hardened audit append buffering/poison handling, and added concurrent append chain verification.

title: FN3 — per-lane telemetry (before W3)
description: FOUNDATION (before W3). Add a per-lane/per-subject label dimension across counters + histograms; a 'blocked' counter; an 'active_lanes' gauge; a per-lane MCP-request latency histogram. Background §4-R: telemetry is process-global bare atomics today. Metrics = a live in-memory ring + append-only per-day history files (FORK-2/D14 — NO TSDB; OTLP export optional). DoD: per-lane metrics emitted; W3 consumes them. Feeds W3/W4 + doctor (DL-10).
close_reason: Implemented per-lane request, blocked, latency, and active-lane telemetry across operator JSON, Prometheus, and OTLP with redacted subject hashes.

title: N-S1 (CRITICAL) — retire the legacy deterministic confirm-MAC
description: CRITICAL. Remove confirmation_mac / verify_commit_confirmation (HMAC over (profile,level,sql), NO caller/lane/nonce — dispatch/mod.rs::confirmation_mac + the compat {sql,token} acceptance, CX-5). The single-use server-side grant (N3) becomes the ONLY confirm path. Why: any lane can recompute another lane's token today → a forgeable cross-lane channel survives even when N9-A4 passes. DoD: confirmation_mac removed; B.5 confirm_mac_retired_cross_lane_recompute_rejected (a lane cannot recompute another's confirm). Depends N3.
close_reason: Retired legacy deterministic confirmation MAC path; session/execute/compile/patch confirmations now use single-use lane-bound grants, with cross-lane rejection tests and docs updated.

title: N-S2 (CRITICAL) — audit ALL committing tools (compile/patch) + classify in compile
description: CRITICAL. Thread AuditCtx into oracle_compile_object + oracle_patch_source and run the classifier in compile. Why: today these commit DDL with NO audit → 'audit = durable record' (N3) is false and W5/W8b/G9 are empty. SEC-3: audit-write failure ⇒ FAIL-CLOSED (refuse the privileged action; the audit sink already fsyncs BEFORE execute — state the contract so no future change makes it best-effort). DoD: every committing tool (execute/compile/patch) appends an audit record; B.5 every_committing_tool_audits; SEC-3 negative test (audit-write-fail refuses). Depends FN2.
close_reason: Implemented N-S2 critical audit coverage: oracle_compile_object now classifies generated compile statements, threads AuditCtx, appends Pending before any DB execute, and appends signed Succeeded/Failed outcomes; oracle_patch_source now appends Pending before generated DDL execution and fails closed on audit append failure before touching Oracle. create_or_replace/deploy_ddl already delegate through audited execute_sql. Added audit_wiring tests for compile + patch Pending/outcome chains and audit-write-failure refusing both before DB execution; updated threat-model evidence. Gates: fmt, oraclemcp tests, workspace clippy -D warnings, workspace tests, cargo deny check, API lock, boundary lint, sensitive-data lint, git diff --check.

title: N-S3 — gate monitor / v$session / db-evidence SQL through the classifier
description: Route the monitor/db-health/sample_rows/dictionary reads + the new operator v$session/W4/G9/monitor_profile SQL through a READ-classified gate + SET TRANSACTION READ ONLY + audit (system Subject), an ALLOW-LIST of parameterized statements (bound params, no caller predicates), output-capped. Why: D6 (same fail-closed core for every face) is currently FALSE for these read-only DBA tools. DoD: these paths are classified+audited+allow-listed; redact other sessions' v$session (N-S6). Depends N0.
close_reason: Gated generated metadata, health, sample, and dictionary reads through classifier allowlist, read-only backstop, and system-subject audit

title: N-S4 — mTLS peer-cert plumbing → CertFingerprint Subject
description: In handle_tls_connection read peer_certificates(), SHA-256 the leaf DER → CertFingerprint; thread it + peer_addr through HttpRequest→DispatchContext→Subject; require a leaf-fp→registered-client map (any CA-signed cert != identity). Why: today the 'mTLS-fp Subject' source does not exist (http.rs never reads peer_certificates). Enables one of the D11 Subject sources. DoD: leaf-fp Subject available; a cert not in the map fails closed. Feeds N7.
close_reason: Implemented mTLS leaf fingerprint principal plumbing with registered-client fail-closed tests.

title: N-S5 — lease stamps server-derived Subject + re-asserts ALTER SESSION allowlist
description: When N0 wires the lease, agent_identity→v$session MUST be the SERVER Subject stable_id (never a tool arg), and the new path RE-CHECKS is_allowed_alter_session itself (do not inherit the lease's trust-the-caller contract, lease.rs:427-437). Why: the dormant oracle_session takes agent_identity as an arg — it must never become the HTTP trust source (D11). DoD: v$session client identity = server Subject; ALTER SESSION re-validated on the lease path. Depends N0.
close_reason: Stamped lease acquire from DispatchContext principal and rechecked ALTER SESSION allowlist in LeaseManager; verified fmt, clippy, tests, deny, lints, and release preflight.

title: N0a(i) — lane runtime/thread ownership (tear out shared dispatch_runtime)
description: STRUCTURAL long-pole. Replace today's single shared current-thread dispatch_runtime (server.rs) with THREAD-PER-LANE: each lane = its own OS thread running its own asupersync current-thread Runtime + reactor + pinned !Send Oracle conn (the proven load/soak pattern; non-Send dispatch futures become a non-issue). Transport = a MT asupersync runtime (Appendix A.1; current_thread()=worker_threads(1) so a lane ≈ 2 OS threads — budget that in CX-I6). C-arch: the LaneRuntime is a REGISTRY OF SEND HANDLES (mailboxes), NOT a map of connections — registry-capability NAME LEASES (Appendix A.8), the leased entry is the Send LaneHandle {mpsc::Sender, generation:u64, cancel, JoinHandle, status}, never the conn. Read path = bounded read-worker lanes / per-reactor shards (NOT one shared OraclePool — reactor-affine, CX-1; and asupersync has NO Pool, A.11). Why: Oracle state is per-session; one shared executor funnels everything. DoD: no HTTP/operator stateful dispatch receives process-global DispatcherState; two blocked lanes don't block a third (N9-C1); LeaseManager reached only via LaneRuntime. Cite Appendix A.1/A.8/A.10.
close_reason: Implemented N0a lane ownership: removed OracleMcpServer's shared dispatch_runtime, added core LaneRuntime as a Send mailbox handle around a dedicated OS-thread/current-thread asupersync runtime+reactor, cloned owned DispatchContext across the mailbox, and wired served HTTP stateful dispatch through served-http-stateful while leaving stdio on the direct dispatcher. Added blocked_lane_does_not_block_another_lane proof that a blocked lane does not block an independent lane; DL-1 prototype/ADR remains as Phase-0 decision evidence. Verification: cargo check -p oraclemcp-core; cargo check -p oraclemcp; cargo test -p oraclemcp-core lane::tests::blocked_lane_does_not_block_another_lane -- --nocapture; cargo test -p oraclemcp --test e2e_http_oauth -- --nocapture; focused core server tests; cargo fmt --all -- --check; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace; cargo deny check; driver/honesty/boundary/api-lock scripts; git diff --check.

title: N-S6 — allow-list-first redaction newtypes for every new surface
description: Allow-list-first redaction for every new surface. (1) BEHAVIOR (0.6.0 default): bind values are REDACTED BY DEFAULT everywhere (audit timeline, proof bundle, logs, telemetry, protocol, UI); an operator REVEAL is explicit and AUDITED (I3 operator-confirmed, supersedes the old FORK-1 operator-sees-everything). There is NO default-off redaction of binds. (2) MECHANISM: redacting newtypes for OracleBind + OracleConnectionInfo; proof bundle/timeline use sql_sha256 NOT sql_preview (or scrub inlined literals, which enter the SIGNED chain today); classify raw ORA- text into a safe envelope; config diff over PARSED allow-listed fields; redact other sessions' v$session; HTML-escape labels/message at render. (3) The configurable RedactionPolicy SEAM (custom per-field/per-role policies, D15) is a future extension and is NOT built for 0.6.0; its absence does not make binds visible (binds stay redacted-by-default regardless). Why: the audit chain is signed, so a leaked literal is permanent. Foundation for N7/W5/W8b/G9/A3/A4. DoD: redaction newtypes; binds redacted by default on EVERY surface; reveal is operator-gated + audited; a test asserts no bind/secret reaches any surface unredacted without an audited reveal. B.11/B.8.
close_reason: Implemented N-S6 redaction foundation: OracleBind now redacts by default in Debug and has explicit redacted serializers for single/positional/named binds; OracleConnectionInfo now redacts Debug plus exposes an allow-listed redacted serializer used by oracle_connection_info protocol output, with identity/topology present only as redacted_fields. Added protocol/type regression tests proving bind and connection sentinels do not reach rendered surfaces; extended sensitive-data lint and docs; refreshed the intentional oraclemcp-db API baseline. Existing reveal work remains with W5 audit timeline; no current surface reveals bind values. Gates: fmt, workspace clippy -D warnings, workspace tests, cargo deny check, API lock, boundary lint, sensitive-data lint, git diff --check.

title: N-S7 (CRITICAL) — bind MCP-session-id to the verified Subject
description: CRITICAL. Every stateful request must assert lane.subject == request.subject; NEVER select a lane by session-id alone. Why: today HttpSessionStore is a bare HashSet<String> and the bearer is validated independently of session membership → any valid bearer can present ANOTHER principal's mcp-session-id and both checks pass = cross-principal lane HIJACK (R2). DoD: B.5/B.6 session_id_bound_to_subject (a valid bearer presenting another principal's session-id is rejected). Depends N0.
close_reason: Bound stateful MCP session ids to the server-derived principal key in HttpSessionStore: initialize records the owner, POST and DELETE validate the current principal before accepting a known mcp-session-id, cross-principal replay returns a generic 403 without leaking subject/token material, and the OAuth stateful E2E now covers the hijack case.

title: C-1 — idle/abandoned-lane reaping via a watchdog (message, never touch the conn)
description: reap_expired (lease.rs:447) has ZERO production callers; expiry is lazy. With N5 (disconnect!=cancel), an abandoned dirty lease holds its txn + row locks until process exit. Design: a WATCHDOG sends a terminate/timeout MESSAGE to the lane's mailbox (a lane parked in a DB call is unblocked by call-timeout/OCI-break per B1; a lane parked idle wakes on its mailbox). A central reaper touching the conn is the C-2 bug — forbidden. DoD: abandoned dirty lane reaped via mailbox, row locks released; B.13#3 abandoned_dirty_lane_reaped_via_mailbox. Depends N5, N0aH.
notes: Implementation landed: stateful HTTP now has a configurable stateful_idle_ttl_seconds defaulting to 900 seconds, with 0 disabling reaping. The HTTP accept loop runs a lightweight reaper on a fixed interval, updates session last_seen on activity, removes expired replay/result buffers, and asks the registered HttpSessionLifecycle to close the session with DispatchCloseReason::Timeout. StatefulLaneDispatch implements that lifecycle by removing the lane and sending the timeout close message to the owning lane; the reaper never touches an Oracle connection directly. Timeout close reasons are propagated into structured lifecycle audit metadata as Timeout/idle_timeout. Config docs and the example TOML describe the new setting and the message-only safety rule. Tests cover idle reaper timeout closure plus buffer cleanup, cross-thread idle mailbox wake, timeout audit metadata, and config loading. Verified by cargo fmt --all -- --check, cargo test -p oraclemcp-core --lib stateful_idle_reaper_closes_by_timeout_and_clears_buffers, cargo test -p oraclemcp-core --lib idle_lane_mailbox_wakes_for_cross_thread_close, cargo test -p oraclemcp --lib lifecycle_timeout_close_audits_timeout_reason, cargo test -p oraclemcp-config --lib http_oauth_config_loads_and_validates, cargo test -p oraclemcp-core --lib, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test --workspace --quiet, cargo deny check, scripts/oraclemcp_api_lock.sh, scripts/oraclemcp_boundary_lint.sh, scripts/sensitive_data_lint.sh, and git diff --check.
close_reason: Implemented idle/abandoned stateful-session reaping by mailbox message: configurable idle TTL, activity tracking, replay-buffer cleanup, Timeout close through HttpSessionLifecycle/StatefulLaneDispatch, and structured Timeout/idle_timeout audit metadata. Reaper never touches the Oracle connection. Verified with focused reaper/wake/audit/config tests, core lib tests, clippy, workspace tests, deny, lints, and diff check.

title: C-4 — Lease/lane epoch (generation u64) + bind grants to it
description: Add a monotonic GENERATION (u64) + lane/subject id to the lane/lease (Lease has none today, lease.rs:144). NOTE (Appendix A.11): this is a PLAIN atomic u64 (published Release, read Acquire — DL-6), NOT asupersync epoch_tracker (which is a scheduler health monitor) and NOT asupersync::epoch (the ATP data layer). Grants (N3) bind to this generation → closes the check-vs-use window on the ceiling (A6/K2/K3). DoD: generation increments on switch_profile/level change; stale grants invalidated; N9-K2/K3. Feeds N3.
close_reason: Added monotonic lane/lease generation primitives and generation-bound execution grant validation. ExecGrantBinding now binds session/lane/subject/generation; stale generation/lane/subject/session mismatch rejects without consuming; LaneRuntime exposes bump_generation; LeaseInfo exposes monotonic generation. Tests: exec_grant, query_execute, lane_generation, acquire_assigns_monotonic_lease_generation. Gates: fmt, affected-crate all-target clippy, api_lock, sensitive_data_lint, boundary_lint, git diff --check.

title: C-5 — switch_profile = atomic conn-swap state machine
description: A lease pins one non-optional conn for life; N1's per-lane switch must ACQUIRE THE NEW profile's permit/conn BEFORE releasing the old (or represent a 'switching' lane state), rolling back to the old on AtCapacity — NEVER strand the lane connection-less. Aligns with K1 'permit released exactly once'. DoD: B.13#3 switch_profile_at_capacity_keeps_old_conn; N9-K1/K2. Depends N1.
close_reason: Made profile switching an explicit prepared-then-commit ownership swap: new connection/stateless/catalog/response preparation completes before dispatcher state is overwritten, commit-side fallible updates run before the infallible swap, and an AT_CAPACITY regression proves the old pinned connection/profile remain usable after a prepared replacement is refused.

title: C-6 — cross-thread cancel of a parked block_on (capability proof)
description: CONFIRM + prove: asupersync can wake a lane parked in block_on on mpsc::recv from another thread (mpsc::Sender::wake_receiver — Appendix A.10, verified for IDLE lanes). A lane INSIDE a blocking Oracle call can only be interrupted by driver OCI-break/call-timeout/socket-close (then treat the conn as suspect → rollback/discard, B1/B1c). asupersync handles idle/async; B1/B1c handle in-flight. DoD: a deterministic test waking an idle parked lane cross-thread; documented in-flight path. Depends N0aH, N5.
notes: Implementation landed while N5 remains in progress: added a deterministic core lane test showing an initialized idle lane parked between mailbox commands wakes and closes from another OS thread via close_with_reason(ServerShutdown), records the close hook reason, and reaches Stopped without external timeout. Documented the split: mailbox wake handles idle lanes; lanes inside Oracle round trips are handled by DB call timeout/driver break/socket close plus quarantine/discard. Verified cargo fmt --all -- --check, cargo test -p oraclemcp-core --lib idle_lane_mailbox_wakes_for_cross_thread_close, cargo test -p oraclemcp-core --lib, cargo clippy -p oraclemcp-core --all-targets -- -D warnings, and git diff --check. br close is currently blocked by dependency oraclemcp-epic-060-f4xo.5.10 (N5), so close this bead after N5 closes or after intentionally relaxing that dependency.
close_reason: Implemented the cross-thread idle wake proof: an initialized idle lane parked between mailbox commands wakes and closes from another OS thread via close_with_reason(ServerShutdown), records the close hook reason, and reaches Stopped. Documented the in-flight Oracle path as call timeout/driver break/socket close plus quarantine/discard. Verified with focused wake/core tests, clippy, workspace tests, deny, lints, and diff check.

title: N-M7 — cross-restart exactly-once (consult durable audit before commit)
description: Execute consults the durable AUDIT CHAIN (sql_sha256 + grant id) BEFORE commit to prevent double-execute across restart; document at-least-once where unavoidable. Ties to CX-C1 (durable write-ahead intent). Why: the in-memory ledger doesn't survive a crash — 're-preview is safe' is FALSE for already-committed DML. DoD: B.6 idempotency_key_dedup_and_no_cross_restart_double_execute. Depends N3.
close_reason: Recovered terminal write-intent history now rejects exact grant+SQL replay across restart; unresolved in-doubt behavior remains fail-closed.

title: CX-I6 (Phase-0 RELEASE-BLOCKER) — measured capacity spike
description: RELEASE-BLOCKER for 0.6.0. The 16/8/64 lane caps (N4) and '51 lanes' (UX) are UNPROVEN for thread-per-lane. Run a measured Phase-0 spike that derives the shipped defaults from real Oracle sessions + fds + systemd TasksMax + per-thread stack memory (remember: a lane ≈ 2 OS threads — current_thread()=worker_threads(1)+the block_on driver, Appendix A.11) + tail latency. The SHIPPED defaults MUST cite this measurement. DoD: B.5 phase0_capacity_spike (--ignored, measured); defaults justified in code + docs. Gates the whole train. Depends N4, N0aR.
close_reason: Implemented CX-I6 live phase0_capacity_spike harness in crates/oraclemcp-core/tests/phase0_capacity.rs and recorded dev-host evidence in docs/performance-footprint.md + tests/artifacts/perf/20260630-cx-i6-phase0-capacity/RESULTS.md. Live run against local Oracle FREE: 16 lane-owned Oracle sessions, 64 probes, p50=1203us p95=1427us p99=1464us max=2090us, observed 2.00 process threads/lane and 4.00 fds/lane, finite resource floor supports global 64 candidate; N4b remains responsible for final shipped defaults. Gates: fmt --check, phase0_capacity --no-run, phase0_capacity ignored live, clippy phase0 test, sensitive_data_lint, boundary_lint, git diff --check.

title: CX-I7 (Phase-0 RELEASE-BLOCKER) — panic-isolation prototype
description: RELEASE-BLOCKER. D12 reverses 0.4.0 panic=abort → unwind + per-lane catch_unwind (Cargo.toml still aborts today). PROTOTYPE + PROVE containment BEFORE relying on it: catch_unwind + Drop + quarantine + audit around the lane block_on loop; a lane panic does NOT abort siblings; the conn is dropped/quarantined; unknown_discarded is audited (CX-2: NO rollback promise after panic). DL-5: use parking_lot/asupersync Mutex (non-poisoning), never std::sync::Mutex, since unwind reintroduces poisoning. DoD: B.5 phase0_panic_isolation (E, real cross-OS-thread). Gates the train. Depends N0aR.
close_reason: Implemented Phase-0 panic isolation: release profile now uses panic=unwind; LaneRuntime catches unwind around the dedicated lane block_on loop, appends a durable lane_panic_unknown_discarded audit record when an auditor exists, logs quarantine, marks the lane Quarantined, and leaves sibling lanes serving. Replaced lane-handle std::sync::Mutex uses with parking_lot non-poisoning locks. Added cross-OS-thread proof lane_panic_is_quarantined_audited_and_sibling_lane_survives plus existing blocked-lane sibling proof. Updated stale abort docs/comments. Verification: cargo test -p oraclemcp-core lane::tests:: -- --nocapture; cargo test -p oraclemcp-guard qualified_udf_call_classify_path_stays_guarded; cargo test -p oraclemcp-telemetry non_ascii_traceparent_is_rejected_not_panicking; cargo check -p oraclemcp; cargo test -p oraclemcp --test e2e_http_oauth -- --nocapture; cargo fmt --all -- --check; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace; cargo deny check; driver/honesty/boundary/api-lock scripts; git diff --check.

title: CX-C1 (Phase-0 RELEASE-BLOCKER) — durable write-ahead idempotency
description: RELEASE-BLOCKER. Grants/idempotency are in-memory (D14/N3) → a committed DML + restart + client retry could DOUBLE-EXECUTE. Before executing any committing tool, append a durable intent{idempotency_key, subject, lane, sql_hash, ts} to an append-only store (the audit chain / an intents file, fsync-before-execute); on restart, an UNRESOLVED intent ⇒ in-doubt → poison/quarantine + surface, NEVER silently re-execute. If we accept at-least-once anywhere, DOCUMENT it explicitly. DoD: B.5 durable_write_ahead_idempotency_across_restart (committed DML + restart + retry → no double-execute). Gates the train. Depends N3; feeds NM7/P1c.
close_reason: Implemented durable write-ahead intent log for committing tools: fsynced pending intents before DB execution, restart refusal on unresolved intents, safe terminal resolution, focused ordering/in-doubt tests, and operator docs.

title: N0a(ii) — connection-thread↔lane-thread handoff (the message-passing seam)
description: STRUCTURAL. The transport (MT runtime) talks to a lane ONLY by message: a bounded mpsc of {command, oneshot reply} (Appendix A.3; two-phase reserve→SendPermit = obligation-tracked send). The conn is reactor-affine to its lane thread, so EVERY conn op (execute/rollback/terminate/reap/switch) is MARSHALED to the owning lane thread via the control mailbox — a central manager NEVER touches a conn directly (fixes C-2). This is where cancel (A.4), DELETE, and SSE plug in. Transport awaits the reply via async oneshot.recv(cx) — NEVER block_on on a worker thread (DL-2). Mailbox full = backpressure/429 (CastOverflowPolicy), never unbounded (DL-9 forbids unbounded). Cite the lane seam Appendix A.10. DoD: a complete command/reply path with cancel/DELETE plumbed; B.5 transport_responsive_while_lane_blocked_in_db.
close_reason: Implemented bounded lane command/reply admission with non-waiting reserve/commit backpressure, pre-admission cancellation checkpoint, HTTP BUSY-to-429 mapping, and B.5 transport responsiveness coverage while a lane is blocked; semantic DELETE/cancel/reap work remains in the existing dependent N5/DL-6/C-6/C-1 beads.

title: SideEffectOracle binding + SELECT-side-effect tightening (Gap-2)
description: Bind the REAL purity oracle from plsql-intelligence into the guard's existing SideEffectOracle port (purity.rs already declares the seam: trait SideEffectOracle{routine_purity, statement_purity}→default Unknown; UnknownOracle is the engine-free default). Implement it over plsql-depgraph + plsql-lineage::column_writers + the trigger/VPD (DBMS_RLS) walk → upgrades the classifier from syntactic+Unknown-fail-closed to SEMANTICALLY-PROVEN-read-only (fewer false refusals; catches SELECT side-effects via triggers/RLS the SQL never names) and UNLOCKS the deferred Gap-2 SELECT tightening (today a UDF-free SELECT stays Safe under the no-engine oracle; tighten to fail-closed-on-Unknown WITH the engine bound). NON-BLOCKING for 0.6.0 (4-RS steer 2): without it the classifier is still fail-closed (Unknown). DoD: oracle bound on the consumer side; SELECT tightening lands WITH it; B.13#1 sideeffect_oracle_binding_never_loosens (binding never turns a refusal into an admission unless proven read-only). Depends A-plsql (engine deps).
close_reason: Bound plsql-intelligence SideEffectOracle consumer-side with SELECT statement-Unknown tightening, trigger/VPD/lineage tests, and guard API baseline. Gates: fmt, clippy workspace, clippy plsql-intelligence, test workspace, plsql feature tests, cargo deny, boundary/arch/agent/driver/concurrency/honesty/sensitive/api/ergonomics/dashboard-skin/installer lints.

title: Capability-secure lanes — cap::None classifier + narrowed lane Cx
description: Make least-privilege STRUCTURAL (type-enforced, not docs). The fail-closed classifier is PURE → cap::None / no Cx (no spawn/io/random/remote reachable from the security decision). Each lane handler receives a NARROWED Cx (typically time + the lane's own io, NEVER HasRemote/HasSpawn). Widening is COMPILE-TIME rejected. Extends the existing A9 surface (cx::{SubsetOf,HasSpawn,HasRemote,HasRandom,CapSetRuntimeMask}) and the ui/ compile-fail tests (widen_narrowed_cx_rejected.rs, read_handler_cannot_spawn_or_remote.rs) PER-LANE. DoD: classifier is cap::None; lane Cx narrowed; per-lane widen-rejected compile-fail tests. Depends N0.
close_reason: Added lane-specific capability narrowing: LaneCaps/narrow_to_lane, lane-loop checkpoints and mailbox receives now run under the narrowed TIME+IO row, and trybuild fixtures prove a lane context cannot call privileged remote effects or widen back to AllCaps. Verified full workspace tests, clippy, deny, API/boundary/concurrency/sensitive/installer/ergonomics gates.

title: DL-4 — canonical lock order + 'never hold registry lock across a lane send'
description: Define oraclemcp's total lock rank and assert ascending acquisition in debug builds with SAFETY: comments: Config(watch, lock-free read) → Registry → Lane(status) → Lease → Grants → Audit-chain → Metadata-cache. HARD RULE: never hold the Registry lock across a lane send — copy the Send LaneHandle out, drop the registry lock, then reserve/send (the 'registry holds only handles' design enables this; codify it). Prefer the actor/single-owner mailbox model so most shared state has no lock-order graph at all; keep locks only where genuinely shared (config via watch, audit-chain via a single writer). DoD: B.13#3 registry_lane_lock_order_ab_ba_unconstructible (AB-BA debug assert). Depends N0aR.
close_reason: Codified the canonical lane lock order, added debug assertions rejecting lane mailbox close/send/drop while the core registry lock is held, documented the copy-handle-before-send invariant, and added the B.13 AB-BA regression.

title: DL-5 — non-poisoning locks + transaction-style audit writer (D12-unwind safe)
description: D12 (unwind + per-lane catch_unwind) reintroduces std::sync::Mutex POISONING risk. Directives: (a) per-lane catch_unwind maps a panic to Outcome::Panicked → supervisor quarantines+restarts the lane (CX-2); (b) use parking_lot::Mutex (non-poisoning) or asupersync Mutex for shared locks — NEVER std::sync::Mutex; (c) the audit-chain writer is TRANSACTION-STYLE (build entry in a local, append-and-swap) so a panic mid-append can't corrupt the chain (protects N-S2). DoD: panic-injection mid-audit-append leaves the chain valid + verifiable; the DL-9 lint forbids std::sync::Mutex in core. Depends FN2.
close_reason: Implemented non-poisoning audit locks and panic-safe audit append poisoning; added panic regression and lint coverage.

title: DL-6 — level-triggered cancel (no lost wakeups)
description: Cancelling an idle lane parked on mpsc::recv must use LEVEL-TRIGGERED desired-state (a cancel/shutdown flag in the LaneHandle, re-read after each recv/on wake), NOT an edge-triggered notify that can be lost if it lands just before the lane parks. mpsc::Sender::wake_receiver wakes the parked lane; epoch generation published Release/read Acquire (or rely on the channel happens-before). asupersync channels already do waker-dedup + capacity-recheck; the APP rule is 'store desired state, recompute on wake'. DoD: B.13#3 lost_wakeup_cancel_one_step_before_park. Depends N0aH.
close_reason: Implemented level-triggered lane close state with wake_receiver and regression coverage for full-mailbox shutdown preemption.

title: N9 — concurrency/session test contract (SET IN STONE)
description: THE WP-N acceptance contract — SET IN STONE; any regression BLOCKS release (DoD item 2). Each invariant = a named test with structured JSON-line logging (lane/subject/SID/profile/level/grant/outcome). Files: extend tests/{chaos,load_soak,cancel_correctness,live_oracle,trust_safety}.rs; ADD tests/lane_state_machine.rs (deterministic model/fault-injection, no Oracle — asupersync lab, Appendix A.13), tests/concurrency_contract.rs (mock, lab injection + seed-sweep + quiescence/obligation-leak/futurelock oracles + crashpack/seed on failure), tests/multi_lane_live_xe.rs (real 23ai, two DBs — no mocks; mock-risk Score>=8 = cross-lane isolation must be mock-free). Groups: A lane isolation (A1-A6 incl grant non-replay + generation binding) · B different DBs · C same-DB contention (readers no head-of-line; same-row write→succeeds or typed CallTimeout, never hangs; deadlock→ORA-00060; MVCC snapshot) · D capacity/fairness (AtCapacity; agent flood can't consume the operator/doctor reserve; effective caps) · E lifecycle (DELETE mid-txn rollback; idle reap; cancelled→dirty-discard; LeaseRequired; clean-cancel-or-quarantine) · F stdio decoupling (byte-identical to 0.4.0; coexist) · G audit under concurrency (per-Subject; valid chain; request_id+idempotency_key_hash → no double record) · H headline e2e (Codex write + Claude read + human dashboard gated, concurrent/isolated; >=50 mixed lanes p50/p95/p99 no leak/starve) · J MCP Streamable-HTTP compliance (400s; SSE ids unique; Last-Event-ID never cross-stream; DELETE drains only target) · K lane state-machine (permit released exactly once across success/error/cancel/timeout/DELETE/reaper; switch increments generation invalidating stale grants; elevation expiry can't race past the ceiling; shutdown drains bounded, audit uncorrupted; NO transition mixes Subject A's conn/grants/audit with B). Changing any invariant needs a plan change + review. Depends the WP-N impl (N1/N2/N3/N5/N6/N7).
close_reason: Implemented N9 concurrency/session contract: WP-N matrix, JSON-line contract test, lane state-machine proofs, live-XE hooks, and conformance accounting gates.

title: N9-edge — lane + classifier edge/negative cases (B.13 #1/#3)
description: Edge/negative companion to N9 (Appendix B.13 #1 classifier + #3 lane concurrency). Classifier (extend adversarial_corpus.rs + proptest_invariants.rs + fuzz/fuzz_targets/classify_fuzz.rs): danger-MONOTONICITY MR (danger(T(sql))>=danger(sql) for danger-adding T: append FOR UPDATE / wrap anonymous block / append ;DROP / writing CTE); reclassification-IDEMPOTENCE MR (classify(sql)==classify(normalize(sql)), normalized form re-classifies the same — the basis for SEC-1/SEC-2); adversarial rows (q'[]'/N''/U&'' literals, full-width/zero-width/RTL keywords, nested /* */, hints, -- eating newline, terminator in a string, unbalanced quote/comment→Forbidden-desync). Lane (extend concurrency_contract.rs + lane_state_machine.rs): lost-wakeup (DL-6), bulkhead permit-leak-on-panic (DL-7), AB-BA lock-order unconstructible (DL-4), switch_profile-at-capacity keeps old conn (C-5), abandoned-dirty-lane reaped via mailbox (C-1). Depends N9.
close_reason: Added B.13 edge/negative test coverage: classifier danger-monotonic and canonical-whitespace idempotence metamorphic checks, Unicode/confusable/desync adversarial rows, panic-terminal lane capacity model, and a JSONL B.13 proof catalog tying existing lane/runtime proofs together. Gates: fmt, clippy workspace, test workspace, focused guard/core tests, cargo deny, boundary/arch/agent/concurrency/honesty/sensitive/api lints.

title: DL-1 (Phase-0) — !Send lane-bridge prototype + decision (gates N0a)
description: Phase-0 spike (section 4-SK.1). Prototype the two !Send-correct lane bridges and pick the least-ambiguous: (a) a dedicated OS thread running its own current-thread Runtime::block_on loop that OWNS the !Send Oracle conn, vs (b) spawn_local / LocalStoredTask on a per-lane local scheduler (Appendix A.10). The real Class-2 hazard is block_on NESTED inside an async task or on a runtime worker thread — so the rule is: the lane block_on is the OUTERMOST call on a DEDICATED lane thread, never nested, never on a transport worker. Document the choice with a SAFETY: comment on the lane-loop entry. The lane thread is a supervised worker under AppSpec; block_on is the local !Send execution bridge, not the app architecture. DoD: a measured prototype of both + a written decision recorded in the repo; the chosen bridge informs N0a. Gates N0a runtime. No deps (run first).
close_reason: DL-1 done: added measured Phase-0 lane bridge prototypes in crates/oraclemcp-core/tests/lane_bridge_phase0.rs; dedicated OS-thread/current-thread-runtime bridge accepted for N0a; spawn_local rejected from consumer code due LocalSchedulerUnavailable/private scheduler TLS; recorded ADR 0007; gates passed: fmt, clippy, workspace tests, cargo deny, driver seam, honesty grep, api lock, boundary lint.

title: D17 — operator-authority allow-list (above Subject, never self-claimed)
description: D17. Operator is an authority CAPABILITY ABOVE a regular authenticated Subject (D11) — required for: fleet-wide view, force-actions on other lanes (R3b), bind REVEAL (FORK-1), Change-Proposal approve/apply + 4-eyes, om doctor --fix, and config/cred management. Sources (NEVER a tool arg): the loopback OS-user / local-pairing owner (single-operator default) OR an explicit operator allow-list (verified Subject stable-ids / OAuth subs / mTLS fps) in config. BINARY in 0.6.0 (operator vs scoped principal); fine-grained operator-RBAC is a deferred non-goal (N4). Every operator action is audited under the Subject; a scoped principal can NEVER escalate to operator. DoD: operator authority resolved from config/owner only; a scoped-principal-cannot-act-as-operator negative test; every operator action audited. Depends N7 (Subject).
close_reason: Implemented server-derived operator authority for /operator/v1 using loopback local-owner or configured subject allow-list, required audit before operator routing, and proved scoped principals cannot self-claim operator.

title: N4b — finalize shipped capacity defaults from the CX-I6 measurement
description: Codex round-4 finding 3: the capacity dependency was backwards — N4's body says the 16/8/64 defaults are JUSTIFIED by CX-I6, but CX-I6 depended on N4, which would let the impl bake defaults before the Phase-0 measurement (§4-CX.1/§7). Fix: N4 (now) = the admission MECHANISM + provisional configurable caps (bulkhead/mpsc token-pool/operator-reserve/AtCapacity) built on N1; CX-I6 = the measured spike (depends on N1 to load-test real lanes); THIS bead = finalize the SHIPPED default numbers from the CX-I6 measurement and cite it in code+docs. DoD: shipped caps cite the CX-I6 measurement; N9-D effective-caps tests use the finalized values. Depends CX-I6, N4.
close_reason: Finalized shipped capacity defaults from CX-I6 measurement and pinned docs/tests to 16/8/64

title: N0 — wire the lease layer behind LaneRuntime
description: Wire the dormant LeaseManager (lease.rs) BEHIND LaneRuntime: each MCP session + verified Subject → a distinct LaneContext + distinct lease; transport never touches LeaseManager directly. The lease is the PHYSICAL session; the lane is the SECURITY+CONCURRENCY boundary. stdio path UNCHANGED (N10). DoD: stateful requests resolve to a LaneContext owning {lane id, verified Subject, MCP-session id, profile, DB fingerprint, level state, grant store, request budget, cancel, audit ctx}; N9-E4 LeaseRequired for a stateful op without a lane.
close_reason: Implemented the N0 stateful lane boundary: HTTP dispatch context now carries MCP-session id plus a server-derived principal key; stateful dispatch fails closed with LeaseRequired without a session-bound context; StatefulLaneDispatch is now a registry of LaneRuntime handles keyed by (MCP-session-id, principal), with dispatcher construction happening on each lane's own runtime via a factory; production stateful HTTP uses that factory-backed registry and stdio remains direct. Per-profile DB/profile isolation, per-lane level state, lane-bound grants, and subject-aware audit remain in the existing dependent N1/N2/N3/N7/N-S* beads.

title: N4c — fair queued stateful admission
description: Residual split from N4 after the pre-connection admission mechanism landed. Implement bounded per-subject queues plus global weighted fairness / brief wait before AT_CAPACITY for stateful lane admission. Keep the current invariants: acquire admission before any lane thread or physical Oracle connection opens; no unbounded queues or waits; operator/doctor reserve cannot be consumed by regular agent flood; diagnostics remain redacted. DoD: N9-D fairness tests prove one subject cannot starve another or the reserved operator/doctor path, and AT_CAPACITY still carries retry_after_ms plus a redacted snapshot.
close_reason: Implemented bounded fair stateful-lane admission: per-subject/global queued caps, round-robin subject turns with brief bounded wait before AT_CAPACITY, permit-drop/drain notifications, and lane allocation fallback that waits outside the registry lock while preserving pre-factory admission. Added fairness/redaction tests and verified full workspace gates.

title: N1 — per-lane connection + per-lane database/profile
description: Each lane owns its conn to the profile IT selected; two lanes → two DBs concurrently; switch_profile affects ONLY the calling lane; the service may hold many DBs at once (pooled per profile, bounded). DoD: N9-B1 (A→X & B→Y concurrent, correct DB each) + N9-B2 (switch_profile affects only A). Depends N0.
close_reason: Implemented N1 coverage: stateful HTTP lanes now prove independent per-lane profile/connection ownership, concurrent A->X and B->Y switches, and A-only later switch isolation.

title: N2 — per-lane operating level + elevation
description: SessionLevelState PER-LANE: A's elevation never affects B; OAuth scope narrows ONLY the calling lane; protected/standby stays READ_ONLY while a sibling is elevated. DoD: N9-A2 (level never leaks) + N9-A3 (elevation TTL independent) + N9-B3 (per-lane ceiling). Depends N0.
close_reason: Verified per-lane operating level isolation over stateful HTTP: the lane factory creates a fresh OracleDispatcher/SessionLevelState per MCP-session+principal lane; a new E2E proves agent A can elevate to READ_WRITE without changing agent B's lane, and a read-scoped request on A's lane sees a lowered effective level without permanently lowering the stored lane state.

title: N3 — single-use, lane-bound grant store (confirm/execute/cursors)
description: Server-side grant store replacing stateless tokens. Preview/confirm mints a grant with {grant_id, lane_id, verified subject_id, MCP-Session-Id, profile_id, profile_revision, DB fingerprint, level GENERATION, SQL SHA-256, classifier-result digest, issued/expiry, action}. The returned token is only an opaque signed reference. EXECUTE CONSUMES THE GRANT EXACTLY ONCE; a retry with the same idempotency key returns the recorded outcome; a second fresh execution → GrantAlreadyConsumed; a grant minted by A is rejected for B, after switch_profile, or after a level/profile generation change. Store + idempotency ledger are in-memory/process-local; the AUDIT HASH-CHAIN is the durable record (a grant lost on restart simply forces re-preview — safe). Builds on the existing guard token.rs AllowOnceStore. DoD: N9-A4 (grant non-replay) + N9-A6 (generation binding); B.5 grant_single_use_lane_bound_generation_bound. Depends N0a handoff.
close_reason: Wired server-side execution grants into the live preview/execute path. LaneRuntime now attaches lane id/generation to DispatchContext; oracle_preview_sql, execute_approved, deploy_ddl, and create_or_replace mint signed opaque references backed by ExecGrantStore; oracle_execute consumes exactly once and validates statement/profile/session/lane/principal/generation before audit/DB execution. Session-level/profile changes bump dispatcher grant generation and clear in-process grants. Added tests for lane-bound non-consumption, generation invalidation, one-shot race, registry routing, and create/deploy compatibility. Verification: fmt, oraclemcp lib tests, oraclemcp-core lib tests, oraclemcp-guard lib tests, affected all-target clippy, api_lock, sensitive_data_lint, boundary_lint, git diff --check.

title: N4 — adaptive bounded capacity + fairness + fail-closed + operator reserve
description: Two-tier caps: per-profile READ pool + stateful/write lanes, plus a global host ceiling. Defaults 16 read / 8 stateful per profile / 64 global (UPPER BOUNDS); startup/doctor compute EFFECTIVE caps = min(configured, DB per-user/session limit, fd limit, memory budget). Reserve >=1 operator + >=1 doctor lane. ACQUIRE A PERMIT BEFORE OPENING A PHYSICAL CONN (Appendix A.9 combinator::bulkhead — obligation-tracked, leak-detected; backed by an mpsc token-bucket; NOTE: asupersync has NO Pool, A.11/DL-7). At capacity → brief wait then typed AtCapacity + retry_after_ms + redacted snapshot — never unbounded sessions. Fairness = per-subject queues + global weighted fairness; idle reaping; DRCP + proxy-auth for scale. DoD: N9-D1..D6; B.5/B.13#3. Depends N1. The 16/8/64 numbers are JUSTIFIED by CX-I6.
close_reason: Implemented the N4 admission mechanism: read-pool default 16, served stateful lane admission before lane thread/Oracle connection creation, 8-per-principal/64-host defaults with operator+doctor regular-capacity reserve, typed AT_CAPACITY retry_after/redacted snapshot, HTTP 429 Retry-After mapping, API baseline, docs, and focused tests. Residual finalized effective caps/default citation, transport accept caps, lifecycle/idle reap, and queued weighted fairness remain tracked by N4b/N4+/N5/N4c and N9.

title: N4+ — bound the accept/connection-thread + SSE layer (transport capacity, CX-4)
description: Codex CX-4: enforce capacity + the operator reserve at the TRANSPORT layer (max in-flight connections + accept backpressure; separately cap + idle-timeout operator/SSE connections — they aren't lanes) BEFORE any Oracle session — else an HTTP/SSE flood starves the operator lane without opening a single DB session. Background: http.rs spawns a thread per accept, uncapped. DoD: accept-layer bounded; SSE subscriber cap; N9-D5 read-flood-can't-starve-operator (at the transport layer too). Depends N0a runtime.
close_reason: Bound HTTP accept workers and Streamable HTTP SSE subscribers with admission caps and redacted backpressure responses.

title: WP-P — Operator protocol (versioned, schema-first)
description: WP-P sub-epic. ONE versioned schema-first protocol over asupersync HTTP: MCP (/mcp, per-lane) + /operator/v1 (read-only stats/events/SSE + gated actions). Generated schema bundle; event-replay (Last-Event-ID in a bounded ring, never cross-lane); idempotency ledger (P1c) + durable write-ahead (CX-C1). Prereqs: FN0 transport/router redesign (precedence, query-string, tool-exec off the POST handler) + FN1 streaming SSE. N-S7 session↔Subject binding. SPA imports generated TS types; CI validates captured fixtures vs the Rust schema. Tests = Appendix B.6. P2 operator UDS DEFERRED.

title: FN0 (STRUCTURAL, before P1) — transport/router redesign
description: STRUCTURAL prereq for the operator surface. Today: a flat exact-match ladder (path not-equal /mcp returns 404, http.rs:1279), the QUERY STRING is discarded (split('?').next(), :1780; HttpRequest has no query field), and the tool call runs SYNCHRONOUSLY inside the POST handler (connection: close, no GET stream, no result buffer). Add: a router with PRECEDENCE (typed-404 API routes vs SPA history-fallback index.html — a mistyped /operator/v1 route must NOT return 200 HTML, R1); QUERY-STRING PARSING for /operator/v1 cursor + W5 filters (R6); move tool execution OFF the POST handler ONTO the lane thread with a result buffer/stream (R3) so N5 disconnect-is-not-cancel + reconnect/resume has a substrate (ties to the N0a handoff + FN1). Also content-type/Accept negotiation + 406 (R7); DELETE on a stateless server returns 405 not a false 202 (R8).
