DoD: router precedence + query parsing + off-handler exec; B.13 part 4 protocol negatives. Prereq for P1/FN1.
close_reason: FN0 transport/router redesign landed: native HTTP now preserves parsed query strings, routes explicitly with reserved typed-json /operator/v1 API 404s, guards operator routes, enforces /mcp Accept and Content-Type negotiation, returns 405 for stateless DELETE, and adds a bounded stateful result buffer with GET replay by cursor/Last-Event-ID. Existing stateful POST behavior remains compatible while dispatch continues through lane runtimes and responses are buffered as the substrate for later SSE/disconnect-resume. Evidence: http.rs protocol-negative/unit tests, conformance coverage update, docs updated; cargo fmt --all -- --check; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace; cargo deny check; API lock; boundary lint; sensitive-data lint; git diff --check.

title: P-rate — per-caller request-rate limiter via asupersync RateLimiter
description: Revives the request-rate half of deferred k6q.11 for the 0.6.0 HTTP/operator ingress. Wrap the served HTTP/operator boundary in asupersync::combinator::RateLimiter keyed only by server-derived, bounded-cardinality principal buckets. It complements N4/N4+ concurrency/backpressure and must not use raw bearer tokens, raw OAuth subs, request IDs, or caller-supplied identity as the limiter key. Out of scope: optimizer/query-cost budgets, which remain deferred separately. DoD: per-principal throttle tests, bounded-cardinality/key-redaction tests, Retry-After/AT_CAPACITY or BUSY mapping, operator/doctor access not starved, docs/behavior-inventory updated.
close_reason: Implemented per-principal HTTP/operator request-rate limiting with bounded redacted buckets, Retry-After/AT_CAPACITY responses, tests, and docs.

title: FN1 (before P1) — streaming SSE transport
description: Real chunked/long-lived response + a server-push channel with a BOUNDED per-subscriber ring buffer + slow-consumer DROP + gap-marker, and Last-Event-ID resume. Today SSE is 2 events in one write (http.rs:1479-1532). Implement with asupersync broadcast/watch (Appendix A.3). P1/P1b/W3/N5 depend on this. SSE GET auth = SameSite=Strict cookie + MANDATORY Origin + guard_http_request + subject-verified Last-Event-ID (EventSource cannot send bearer/CSRF). DoD: streaming SSE + bounded ring + resume; B.13 part 4 sse-slow-drop. Depends FN0.
notes: 2026-06-30 final implementation: served GET /mcp SSE now uses HTTP/1.1 Transfer-Encoding: chunked and stays long-lived. HttpResultStore provides a bounded 128-event retained ring per session; connected subscribers wait on append/delete/shutdown, replay from cursor/Last-Event-ID, and receive event: stream-gap when a slow consumer resumes behind retention. Query cursor expiry still fails closed with 410. Stateful initialize now also issues an HttpOnly SameSite=Strict oraclemcp_mcp_session cookie. Cookie-authenticated SSE GET requires Origin, still passes guard_http_request Origin allowlisting, can replay under OAuth without Authorization for EventSource, and header-only GET without bearer remains 401. Implementation intentionally uses the existing blocking native transport plus Condvar wakeups instead of adding new async/web deps; boundary lint confirms tokio/hyper/reqwest remain absent. Proofs added/extended: served_stateful_get_streams_chunked_sse_until_session_closes, stateful_get replay/expiry/gap tests, stateful_initialize_sets_strict_session_cookie, oauth_stateful_get_accepts_strict_cookie_with_origin_only, golden HTTP behavior, and served OAuth stateful tests. Gates passed: cargo fmt --all -- --check; cargo test -p oraclemcp-core --lib; cargo test -p oraclemcp-core --test golden_behavior -- http; cargo test -p oraclemcp --test e2e_http_oauth -- stateful; cargo clippy -p oraclemcp-core --all-targets -- -D warnings; scripts/oraclemcp_boundary_lint.sh; scripts/sensitive_data_lint.sh; scripts/oraclemcp_api_lock.sh; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace --quiet; cargo deny check; git diff --check.
close_reason: Implemented streaming SSE transport: chunked long-lived GET, bounded retained event ring with slow-consumer stream-gap and Last-Event-ID resume, shutdown/delete wakeups, and EventSource cookie/Origin OAuth path; full gates passed.

title: P1 — define + version /mcp + /operator/v1 (schema-first)
description: Define the versioned protocol: (a) MCP for agents (per-lane); honors MCP-Protocol-Version (unsupported returns typed 400). (b) read-only OPERATOR channel — metrics/health/audit-tail/active-lanes + optional v$session summary (redacted, privilege-scoped, source = self_lane|monitor_profile|unavailable) via SSE + REST under /operator/v1. (c) GATED-ACTION routes (preview/confirm/execute, set-level, switch-profile) mapping to the same per-lane guarded dispatch. Carries a protocol version + a GENERATED machine-readable schema bundle (operator.schema.json + route/event schemas); every event has event_seq, event_id, lane_id, subject_id_hash, redaction_level, schema_version. The SPA imports generated TS types; CI validates captured UI fixtures against the Rust schema. DoD: B.6 mcp_and_operator_v1_conformance_matrix (MUST coverage at least 0.95) + ui_fixtures_validate_against_rust_schema. Depends FN0, FN1 (and WP-N — cross).
close_reason: Implemented versioned MCP/operator v1 protocol, generated schema and TypeScript artifacts, UI fixture validation, and B.6 conformance matrix.

title: P1b — event replay/resume (bounded ring, never cross-lane)
description: /operator/v1/events supports Last-Event-ID/cursor resume within a bounded ring buffer; NEVER replays another subject/lane; payloads stay redacted. DoD: B.6 sse_replay_never_crosses_lane. Depends P1.
close_reason: Implemented bounded /operator/v1/events replay/resume with subject+lane isolation, stale cursor handling, docs, and conformance coverage.

title: P1c — idempotency ledger for gated actions
description: preview/confirm/execute/commit/rollback/set-level/switch-profile take or derive an idempotency key; the ledger stores request id, lane, subject, grant, SQL hash, audit seq, timestamps, outcome. Same key returns the original result or a typed in-progress; a different key cannot reuse a consumed grant. Define key DERIVATION for non-SQL actions (set-level/switch-profile) with a generation/sequence component. Pairs with CX-C1 (durable write-ahead) + N-M7 (consult the durable audit before commit) so a committed DML cannot double-execute across restart. DoD: B.6 idempotency_key_dedup_and_no_cross_restart_double_execute. Depends P1 (+ CX-C1/N3 cross).
close_reason: Implemented operator v1 gated-action idempotency: explicit/derived keys, in-progress/conflict responses, same-key replay without re-dispatch, generated UI fixture/types, and B.6 coverage.

title: P-Outcome — preserve asupersync Outcome to the HTTP edge
description: From section 4-AS.2: carry asupersync Outcome (the four-valued severity lattice Ok then Err then Cancelled then Panicked, Appendix A.6) lane to dispatch to transport, collapsing ONLY at the MCP/HTTP policy boundary: Cancelled maps to 499, Panicked maps to 500 plus page-supervision, Err maps to a domain error. Do NOT flatten to a plain Result-of-String at the first adapter. Why: Cancelled (client gone / shutdown / timeout) is NOT an error — it changes audit/retry/drain; Panicked must page. DoD: Outcome preserved end-to-end; the boundary mapping tested. Depends P1.
close_reason: Preserved asupersync Outcome through dispatch lanes and mapped HTTP boundary outcomes.

title: MCP-design hardening (MCP-1..MCP-14)
description: Agent theory-of-mind hardening for the MCP surface (section 4-SK.2): educational STRUCTURED refusals (why-blocked + safe alternative, never a bare error); capability-gated tools/list by effective ceiling + tools/list_changed on level/profile change; discovery resources (capabilities/tool docs); an escalation macro that NEVER auto-confirms; DEVIATION MCP-13 = coerce-cosmetic / strict-on-safety (overrides the skill forgive-by-default for safety-relevant args); oracle_capabilities (server-direct path) must report the CALLING lane level/profile/DB. DoD: refusals carry next-steps; tools/list reflects the effective ceiling; B.6/B.11. Depends P1.
close_reason: Implemented dynamic MCP discovery surface: tools/list and discovery resources/capabilities now reflect calling lane level/profile/OAuth ceiling; lane wrappers forward surface snapshots; tests/goldens cover read-only, scoped, elevated, and static dispatcher surfaces.

title: Tests B.6 — WP-P protocol acceptance suite
description: Appendix B.6. mcp_and_operator_v1_conformance_matrix (C | mcp_conformance extended with /operator/v1; spec-derived MUST/SHOULD, MUST coverage at least 0.95, MCP-Protocol-Version/MCP-Session-Id missing/unsupported → typed 400, DISCREPANCIES.md); ui_fixtures_validate_against_rust_schema (C/G | tests/golden; operator.schema.json + event envelope event_seq/event_id/lane_id/subject_id_hash/redaction_level/schema_version); sse_replay_never_crosses_lane (P/E | e2e_http_oauth); idempotency_key_dedup_and_no_cross_restart_double_execute (E | multi_lane_live_xe); session_id_bound_to_subject (U/E | trust_safety; N-S7). Plus B.13#4 protocol negatives: malformed JSON-RPC → typed not panic; oversized → bounded; 406 (R7); 405 not false-202 (R8); typed-404 vs SPA fallback precedence (R1); SSE slow-consumer drop+gap (FN1). DoD: all green. Depends P1,P1b,P1c,FN0,FN1,MCPD.
close_reason: B.6 conformance scenario now runs the cited MCP, HTTP/operator, OAuth/session, structured-schema, and durable write-intent restart proof suites; coverage matrix includes durable SQL idempotency.

title: DEFERRED — operator Unix-domain socket (P2; future native client)
description: DEFERRED idea (plan P2; N12 0.6.0 ships loopback-TCP-only). A local operator Unix-domain socket for a future native client. 0.6.0 has no consumer (browser=TCP, stdio=pipes, TUI deferred) so keep the operator transport ABSTRACTED so a UDS drops in later with NO protocol change. Trigger: a native local CLI/TUI that needs UDS. Depends P1.

title: WP-S — Persistent always-on service
description: WP-S sub-epic. Service = an asupersync AppSpec supervision tree (A.7): children transport/lane-registry-supervisor/audit-writer/metrics/dashboard-API; RestForOne ordered (audit-writer before transport), OneForOne lanes; AppHandle as obligation (stop→join, never drop). service install|uninstall|status|logs|restart (systemd --user + enable-linger / launchd / Windows); sd_notify(READY=1)→/readyz; single-instance guard (no silent takeover); mimalloc; safe config reload/drain (S5); file-store crash/concurrency/path-safety (CX-I5); om backup/restore (§4-GT.6). Persistence files-first, pure-Rust, NEVER SQLite (D14). Tests = Appendix B.7.
close_reason: All WP-S implementation and B.7 acceptance beads are closed; full workspace gates and focused service acceptance checks pass.

title: S-AppSpec — model the service as an asupersync AppSpec supervision tree
description: From section 4-AS.2: the always-on service is an AppSpec supervision tree (root region), NOT a bare RuntimeBuilder+block_on (Appendix A.7). Children: transport (HTTP/SSE accept), lane-registry/supervisor, audit-chain writer, metrics/health collector, dashboard-API. RestartPolicy encodes dependency shape: lanes independent use OneForOne; ordered children (audit-writer before transport accepts; registry before lanes) use RestForOne; SupervisionStrategy (Stop, Restart(RestartConfig{max_restarts,window,backoff}), or Escalate) is a SEPARATE decision from RestartPolicy. The lane registry = registry-capability NAME LEASES (A.8), not an ambient HashMap. Hold the AppHandle as an OBLIGATION — graceful shutdown (S5) = AppHandle::stop then join, NEVER drop. DoD: B.7 appspec_topology_starts_in_dep_order_and_drains (lab); removes the hand-rolled startup/shutdown + watchdog. Depends N0aR (cross).
notes: S-AppSpec progress extended: the service_app module now exposes ServiceAppRuntime plus ServiceAppStartError and start_oraclemcp_service_app(), using RuntimeBuilder::current_thread().block_on to obtain a production-visible current Cx instead of the test-only Cx::new seam. HTTP/HTTPS serve starts this AppSpec obligation before binding and always calls AppHandle::stop then join after the transport loop exits or errors; startup/shutdown failures are reported fail-closed. Added service_app_runtime_resolves_app_handle_obligation alongside appspec_topology_starts_in_dep_order_and_drains, so the lab now covers topology order, RestForOne restart suffixes, registry propagation, synthetic drain, and the public start/stop wrapper. Verified cargo fmt --all -- --check, cargo test -p oraclemcp-core --lib service_app, cargo clippy -p oraclemcp-core --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo clippy -p oraclemcp --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings, scripts/oraclemcp_boundary_lint.sh, scripts/sensitive_data_lint.sh, scripts/oraclemcp_api_lock.sh, cargo test --workspace --quiet, cargo deny check, and git diff --check. Still not closed: the HTTP accept loop and idle reaper remain native code outside an asupersync transport child; S-AppSpec is not complete until the transport child owns that lifecycle or the bead is deliberately narrowed.
close_reason: Implemented S-AppSpec: service_app now defines the oraclemcp-service AppSpec topology with audit, metrics, lane-registry, dashboard, and transport children; RestForOne ordering and per-child strategies are covered by appspec_topology_starts_in_dep_order_and_drains. Production HTTP/HTTPS serve now binds first, hands the listener to ServiceTransport, starts it via start_oraclemcp_service_app_with_transport as the AppSpec transport child, waits on the transport child, then resolves the AppHandle obligation with stop_and_join; main no longer calls the serve loop directly. Added tests for public AppHandle start/stop and AppSpec-owned HTTP accept-loop shutdown. Verified cargo fmt --all -- --check, cargo test -p oraclemcp-core --lib service_app, cargo clippy -p oraclemcp-core --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo clippy -p oraclemcp --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings, scripts/oraclemcp_boundary_lint.sh, scripts/sensitive_data_lint.sh, scripts/oraclemcp_api_lock.sh, cargo test --workspace --quiet, cargo deny check, and git diff --check.

title: S-unit — systemd unit hardening (limits for 64+ lane-threads)
description: systemd unit hardening: LimitNOFILE, TasksMax, MemoryMax, Type=notify/NotifyAccess, Restart=on-failure, OOMScoreAdjust; doctor reports effective-vs-configured caps; launchd/Windows equivalents. Why: 64+ lane-threads (about 2 OS threads per lane) vs default user.slice limits would silently throttle N4. DoD: hardened units; doctor surfaces effective caps (N9-D6). Depends S1.
close_reason: Implemented service-unit hardening across systemd, launchd, and Windows dry-run/install plans; doctor now reports configured service caps and effective current process/cgroup caps.

title: Tests B.7 — WP-S service acceptance suite
description: Appendix B.7. appspec_topology_starts_in_dep_order_and_drains (L | service_topology; RestForOne ordering, AppHandle stop→join bounded, N9-K4); service_lifecycle_e2e (E | new service_e2e, --ignored; install/uninstall/status/logs/restart, sd_notify→/readyz, single-instance fail-closed); safe_config_reload_drains_not_drops (E | service_e2e; S5); file_store_atomic_fsync_lock_path_safe (U/P | new file_store; CX-I5 atomic+fsync+lock+torn-tail+path-safety); backup_restore_verifies_audit_chain (E | file_store; SEC-1 re-enforce). DoD: all green. Depends SAPP,S1,S5,SFILE,SBAK.
close_reason: Verified the WP-S B.7 acceptance suite is green: AppSpec topology, service lifecycle and single-instance planning, readyz, S5 drain, file-store atomic/path safety, and backup/restore audit verification all pass. Full workspace gate also passed.

title: D18 — SecretResolver seam (Oracle creds = external refs, never persisted/rendered)
description: D18. Oracle DB credentials are EXTERNAL REFERENCES resolved at connect, never stored by oraclemcp. The profile config holds a reference — an env-var name, a file path (wallet dir / key file), or an OS-keyring entry — resolved at connect via a pluggable SecretResolver seam (D15 rule 2: trait seam where change is expected; future vault/OCI-secrets plug in). The raw secret never enters the config file, audit chain, logs, telemetry, protocol, or UI (the N-S6 redaction newtypes enforce non-serialization). Generalizes the existing credential_ref/wallet_password_ref (env:/literal:) resolution. Literal credentials rejected on protected profiles. DoD: SecretResolver trait + env/file/keyring impls; a test that no resolved secret is serialized into ANY surface; protected rejects literal. No deps (config/db layer).
close_reason: Implemented SecretResolver seam and threaded connect-time resolution through profile, audit, HTTP, and lane paths; added redaction/leak tests and sensitive-data lint coverage.

title: 0.4.x to 0.6.0 migration (additive config + audit format_version + doctor-assisted)
description: The 0.4.x to 0.6.0 migration story (section 4-GT.6). Config is additive/schema-first (D15): new fields (monitor_profile, dashboard_ddl_workbench, dashboard_workbench) bump schema_version with a migration note. The audit-chain JSONL is append-only + format-versioned: the existing chain continues; a format_version guards any change — NEVER rewrite (DOC-5). New state dirs (the XDG state dir oraclemcp) are created on first run. om doctor detects a LEGACY layout and OFFERS migration (doctor-assisted, scoped, reversible). All file formats follow the same additive discipline so each train upgrades in place. DoD: a clean 0.4.x to 0.6.0 upgrade with no data loss; doctor detects+migrates a legacy layout; an upgrade test covers it. Depends SFILE (new layout), FN2 (audit format_version).
close_reason: Implemented additive config schema v2 fields, XDG audit default migration, and doctor-assisted legacy audit layout migration

title: D18 residual — lazy SecretResolver connect-path plumbing
description: Residual D18 plumbing after the SecretResolver seam already existed: keep profile credential_ref and wallet_password_ref as external references during startup/profile selection and resolve through the injected SecretResolver only when opening a real Oracle connection: stdio/stateless startup connect, readiness probe connect, and stateful per-lane connect. Keep existing env/file/keyring/literal/vault resolver seam; no new deps.
close_reason: Kept profile secret refs lazy through startup metadata selection and resolved them only inside connection openers.

title: S1 — service install|uninstall|status|logs|restart
description: The service CLI: systemd --user + loginctl enable-linger / launchd / Windows service. Why: reboot-surviving always-on (G3). DoD: all five subcommands; reboot then service running; B.7 service_lifecycle_e2e (--ignored). Depends P1 (cross), SAPP.
close_reason: Implemented service install/uninstall/status/logs/restart CLI with explicit --yes confirmation, dry-run JSON plans, systemd --user/launchd/Windows service-manager command plans, README and robot-docs guidance, and focused tests. Verified fmt, clippy, workspace tests, deny, release preflight, sensitive lint, API lock, and git diff --check.

title: S2 — sd_notify(READY=1) + /readyz
description: sd_notify(READY=1) on startup + a /readyz health endpoint (the installer health-gate, D8). SEC-5: /readyz must NOT leak (no v$session/metadata on this unauth surface). DoD: readyz returns ready only when bound+healthy; B.7. Depends S1.
close_reason: Implemented dependency-free systemd READY=1 notification after the HTTP service AppSpec starts; /readyz remains DB-gated.

title: S3 — single-instance guard + discovery (no silent takeover)
description: Single-instance guard + discovery so a 2nd instance FAILS CLOSED (no silent takeover of the port/socket). DoD: a 2nd instance refuses; B.7 single-instance guard. Depends S1.
close_reason: Implemented HTTP service runtime instance guard with pid/listen discovery, status reporting, tests, and docs.

title: S4 — mimalloc + gated background workers + documented lock hierarchy
description: mimalloc allocator + any background workers behind explicit gates + the documented lock hierarchy (DL-4 rank: Config then Registry then Lane then Lease then Grants then Audit then Metadata). DoD: allocator wired; lock hierarchy documented + debug-asserted (shared with DL-4). Depends S1.
close_reason: Wired mimalloc behind an explicit opt-in feature, documented gated service workers and DL-4 lock hierarchy, and added S4 conformance accounting backed by existing worker/lock tests.

title: S5 — safe config reload/drain
description: A reload does NOT drop active lanes unless a profile changed INCOMPATIBLY; removed/changed profiles DRAIN (no new lanes; existing expire or are explicitly terminated). Per-client cred revoke/rotate (E-cred) joins the reload set, causing immediate lane teardown (N5) + grant revocation. DoD: B.7 safe_config_reload_drains_not_drops. Depends S1, SAPP.
close_reason: Implemented validated config reload planning and profile-scoped drain admission: compatible metadata changes retain lanes, removed/incompatible profiles drain, top-level HTTP/audit/default-profile changes require restart, and served profile list/switch/active work fail closed for drained profiles.

title: S-cfg — extract the config-ops backend (draft to validate to diff to atomic to reload)
description: Extract the config-ops backend into rc1 so E5 setup --write does not depend on rc2 W2: stage a DRAFT, validate with the REAL strict config loader + redaction tests, optional doctor --profile, REDACTED diff (over parsed allow-listed fields, N-S6), ATOMIC-RENAME write with timestamped backup, ask the service to reload (S5), rollback restores+revalidates. Shared by W2 (dashboard) + E5 (setup --write). DoD: backend covered by tests; W2/E5 consume it. Depends S5 (reload), SFILE (atomic write).
close_reason: Implemented reusable config-ops backend in oraclemcp-core: strict draft validation via OracleMcpConfig, redacted metadata diff, service-lock guarded backup + atomic replace, S5 reload plan output, rollback from timestamped backup, race detection, and secret-redaction tests. Gates: cargo fmt --all -- --check; git diff --check; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace; cargo deny check; scripts/oraclemcp_driver_seam_lint.sh; scripts/oraclemcp_honesty_grep.sh; scripts/oraclemcp_api_lock.sh; scripts/oraclemcp_boundary_lint.sh; scripts/oraclemcp_concurrency_lint.sh; scripts/sensitive_data_lint.sh.

title: S-file — file-store crash/concurrency/security contract (CX-I5)
description: D14 persistence = files-first, pure-Rust, NEVER SQLite. The file-store contract (CX-I5): atomic write-tmp-then-rename + fsync(file) + fsync(dir); a MANDATORY single-writer SERVICE LOCK for every mutation; CRASH RECOVERY (skip/repair a torn tail, rebuild the index); retention/rotation for prunable data (metrics; NEVER audit); PATH SAFETY — never interpolate untrusted profile/author names into a path; use sanitized, length-bounded, or content-HASHED IDs (the proposals dir keyed by profile and author is otherwise a traversal vector). Layout under the XDG state dir oraclemcp. DoD: B.7 file_store_atomic_fsync_lock_path_safe. Foundation for W2/SCFG/SBAK/audit/metrics/proposals.
close_reason: Added oraclemcp-core file_store foundation: XDG state root, content-hashed StoreId, service lock token, atomic temp/fsync/rename/fsync-dir writes, JSONL tail recovery/index rebuild, prunable retention with audit refusal, path/permission tests, and operations docs.

title: S-backup — om backup / om restore (state dir + config)
description: om backup snapshots the XDG state dir oraclemcp + config (consistent: take the service lock / drain S5 first). om restore = stop, restore dir, start. The audit hash-chain lets restore VERIFY integrity (and re-enforce — SEC-1: restore is a recovery path, re-run classifier+level+audit, never trust stored verdicts). Metrics files are prunable by retention (CX-I5); the audit chain is never pruned. DoD: B.7 backup_restore_verifies_audit_chain. Depends SFILE.
close_reason: Implemented service backup/restore with audit-chain verification, docs, and B.7 coverage.

title: WP-W — Web dashboard (PRIMARY human front-end)
description: WP-W sub-epic. React+Vite SPA (TanStack + shadcn + Tailwind + CodeMirror + three.js/r3f + GSAP), build-time only, embedded via rust-embed over asupersync HTTP. Phased: 0.6.0 read-only control plane (Overview/Sessions/Audit/Doctor, 2D, skinnable seam); 0.6.1 full views + governed Workbench (W8, guard-is-the-feature, no fail-closed exception) + plsql-intelligence + version history; 0.6.2 Change-Review board ('PR for PL/SQL') + 3D Orrery skin + migration export. Browser-safe auth (D10): local pairing on loopback, HttpOnly/SameSite cookie, CSRF+Origin per POST, CSP/frame-ancestors. Skinnable (D16): view-model/skin/theme/renderer seam, three.js quarantined, mandatory 2D/no-WebGL fallback, grammar-is-a-contract. SEC-1 CP-apply re-classifies. Carries §4-WD.1-.8. Tests = Appendix B.8.
close_reason: Dashboard work package complete: shipped implementation and acceptance beads are closed; WD-Orrery and ftui are explicitly deferred/out of scope for this closeout.

title: W0 — scaffold the SPA + embed pipeline
description: React + Vite + TanStack (Router/Query/Table) + shadcn + Tailwind, CLIENT SPA (NO TanStack Start/SSR — SSR is a 2nd daemon, forbidden). CI build produces a static bundle embedded via rust-embed (default-features-off, no web-framework feature → no boundary-lint trip; add to deny.toml) served over the asupersync HTTP. npm audit + lockfile/SBOM alongside cargo-deny; --skip-build uses the embedded bundle. D13: dashboard-api default-on-but-inert; dashboard-bundle NOT default (product artifacts ship it via the E0 web-build job). DoD: B.8 embedded_bundle_served_and_audited. Depends P1 (cross).
close_reason: Upgraded the dashboard scaffold from vanilla TypeScript to a React/Vite client SPA with TanStack Router, Query, and Table, Tailwind via the Vite plugin, local shadcn-style primitives, exact-pinned npm deps, dev/preview scripts, SBOM generation, and bundle-check enforcement. Preserved the existing rust-embed dashboard-bundle feature and verified the generated bundle is served without API fallback.

title: W8b — proof bundle for gated actions
description: A redacted proof bundle for gated actions: audit seq/hash, SQL hash, classifier decision, subject, lane, DB fingerprint, session tags/SID when available, outcome — NO bind values/secrets (N-S6). DoD: exportable proof bundle; B.8/B.11. Depends W8, W5.
close_reason: Existing operator audit-tail proof bundle verified and B.8/B.11 accounting pinned: server redaction test audit_tail_filters_exports_redacted_proof_bundle, dashboard acceptance w8b_proof_bundle_is_redacted_and_exportable, conformance totals updated to 70 MUST / 72 tested. Gates: fmt, clippy, cargo test --workspace, cargo deny, driver seam, honesty, sensitive, boundary, dashboard skin, conformance dry-runs, manual api lock.

title: W9 — optional read-only browser mirror of health/stats
description: An optional read-only browser mirror of health/stats (low-priority convenience). DoD: read-only mirror behind a flag; B.8. Depends W1.
close_reason: Verified W9 is satisfied by the existing dashboard Overview/Health/Capacity read-only mirror over /operator/v1/health and /operator/v1/metrics, gated by the dashboard-bundle Cargo feature plus paired dashboard session. Added explicit W9 regression coverage, B.8/e2e accounting, and docs. Gates: fmt, focused dashboard tests, workspace clippy -D warnings, workspace tests, cargo deny, sensitive-data lint, boundary lint, driver seam lint, honesty grep, agent surface lint, API lock, mcp/operator conformance dry-run, dashboard_readonly dry-run, web npm check.

title: W10 — client-credentials screen
description: List registered MCP clients (scopes, created/last-used, last source address); revoke/rotate ONE without affecting others; NO token value shown after creation (FORK-3 — these are oraclemcp own per-client HTTP creds, hashed at rest, shown once; never Oracle DB creds). Revoke/rotate joins the S5 reload set → immediate lane teardown (N5) + grant revocation. DoD: B.8 + B.9 per_client_scoped_creds_isolated. Depends W1, E-cred (cross).
close_reason: Implemented W10 client-credentials dashboard/operator routes with redacted list, one-time rotate bearer, revoke/rotate principal cleanup, schema fixtures, and conformance coverage.

title: WD-Identity — Ground Control identity + signatures (2D first)
description: Section 4-WD.7 identity Ground Control (Apollo mission-control). Signatures: GO/NO-GO, the Clearance Ladder (READ_ONLY..ADMIN), the Countdown (elevation TTL), the Logbook (audit). 5 legibility principles: fixed grammar (position=structure, color=clearance, motion=activity), calm-by-default, plain-language chrome, progressive disclosure, instrument-grade restraint. The visual GRAMMAR is a contract invariant across all skins — comprehension never depends on the skin. 0.6.0 ships the 2D realization; the 3D Orrery is the 0.6.2 skin. DoD: signatures + grammar implemented in 2D; B.8. Depends W1.
close_reason: Implemented the 2D Ground Control identity strip with GO/NO-GO, clearance ladder, countdown, and logbook signatures wired to existing operator health, metrics, and audit endpoints.

title: WD-Skin — skinnable presentation architecture (D16)
description: D16/section 4-WD.8: separate a stable semantic VIEW-MODEL (from the protocol/resources) from a swappable SKIN (named preset = theme + per-surface renderer choice + overrides) + THEME (CSS vars + WebGL uniforms). A BigBoardRenderer seam swaps the hero surface (Orrery-3D / 2D-board / table) by capability. three.js is QUARANTINED in the OrreryRenderer (lazy/code-split). The grammar is a CONTRACT across skins; skins-pure dep-lint + a skin-conformance CI test; a MANDATORY 2D/no-WebGL fallback + the a11y suite (light/dark/colorblind/high-contrast/reduced-motion). Built-in code-split skins in 0.6.0; runtime/third-party skins deferred (security review). DoD: B.8 skin_conformance_2d_fallback_a11y. Depends WDID.
close_reason: Implemented D16 dashboard presentation contract: semantic view models, Ground Control skin/theme, 2D/table BigBoard fallback, lazy Orrery boundary, skin lint, and web/release gates.

title: WD-Orrery — full 3D Orrery renderer (three.js/r3f + GSAP)
description: DEFERRED (2026-07-02, operator): the 3D three.js/r3f Orrery (star + orbiting bodies) is DROPPED as the hero — the star/orbit metaphor was not compelling. The 2D BigBoard hero (already shipped via WD-Identity + WD-Skin, and the mandatory a11y/no-WebGL fallback) is now the DEFAULT hero for 0.6.x; this SUPERSEDES I11 (3D-default). A new hero SIGNATURE is being designed in Claude Design (candidates: ATC strip-board, departures Big-Board, rack/datacenter, terminal deck, bridge, reactor) and will be added as a separate WD-Signature bead that drops into the BigBoardRenderer seam. This 3D Orrery stays a POSSIBLE future optional skin-renderer (like the deferred ftui-TUI / UDS ideas) behind the same seam — do NOT build for 0.6.x unless re-triggered. Grammar unchanged (position=structure, color=clearance, motion=activity, GO/NO-GO, ladder, countdown, logbook).

title: WD-IDE — plsql-intelligence IDE features in the Workbench
description: Wire the plsql-intelligence ENGINE crates into the Workbench (0.6.1): go-to-def, find-usages, dependency graph, lint (plsql-sast), refactor across packages/functions/triggers/views. Consume plsql-core/ir/parser-antlr/depgraph/symbols/lineage/sast ONLY (offline, pure). Depends on the engine deps (A-plsql). DoD: IDE features live in the Workbench; B.8. Depends W8, A-plsql (cross).
close_reason: Implemented Workbench PL/SQL IDE wiring: static oracle_plsql_* tools are available through the operator execute route, parse/analyze responses expose bounded IDE navigation and graph summaries, and the Workbench has parse/docs/analyze/lineage/SAST/impact actions plus local usage and rename preview. Verified with dashboard check, feature/static tests, full workspace clippy/test, deny, and lints.

title: WD-Search — global DB search (all object types)
description: Global search across everything in the database (all object types, toggle) via the read-classified gated dictionary/source-search tools. Operator-requested wow feature. DoD: global search across object types; B.8. Depends W7.
close_reason: Implemented Explorer global object/source search through guarded dictionary tools; documented coverage and gates passed.

title: WD-History — object version-history + revert
description: Object version-history + revert: each governed edit snapshots the prior source (DDL source-replaceable objects), enabling WD-RULE-1 DDL revert. Snapshots are content-addressed files (D14/SFILE). DoD: version history + source-snapshot revert; B.8. Depends W8, SFILE (cross).
close_reason: Implemented source-history snapshots for governed source-replaceable DDL, source-free history listing, and revert drafting through change proposals; verified focused tests plus full gates.

title: WD-PR — Change-Review board (PR for PL/SQL) + SEC-1 apply re-classify
description: The 0.6.2 Change-Review board (PR for PL/SQL), Git+Cursor-informed, keyed by (profile, author=agent|human); multiple named Change Proposals; surfaced in Mission Control + a global Reviews filter; one CP = one profile; D5 visibility; proposed CPs are stateless on disk (a lane is acquired only at apply). Apply unit = per-object (DDL) / per-statement-or-grouped-txn (DML); honest atomicity (multi-DDL sequential/stop-on-failure + per-object snapshot-revert; no false all-or-nothing); agent DML stored as parameterized SQL + captured binds (never literals; classifier classifies the template). SEC-1 (CRITICAL recovery path): apply RE-CLASSIFIES + re-checks level/grants/Subject at apply time — NEVER trusts the stored proposal verdict. DoD: B.8 cp_apply_reclassifies_never_trusts_stored_verdict. Depends W8, W5.
close_reason: Implemented Change Review board with apply-time reclassification and guarded action routing

title: W1 — shell + browser-safe auth (D10)
description: Local pairing/auth EVEN ON LOOPBACK (loopback protects the network, not the browser origin). om dashboard opens a one-time local ticket URL (a 0600 token in the XDG runtime dir, single-use, exchanged in 60s for an HttpOnly+SameSite=Strict session cookie then invalidated; Referrer-Policy: no-referrer; no token in localStorage or URLs). Every dashboard POST requires Origin/Host validation + a CSRF token + a scoped action ticket; CSP, frame-ancestors none, nosniff, referrer-policy. Cred to Subject re-verified PER REQUEST (never session-id alone, N-S7). DoD: B.8 malicious_page_cannot_trigger_gated_action (CSRF/Origin/cookie/clickjacking). Depends W0, P1.
close_reason: Implemented dashboard local pairing/session auth: 60s single-use runtime ticket, HttpOnly SameSite cookie, CSRF + route-scoped action tickets for dashboard POSTs, same-origin guards, CSP/frame/nosniff/referrer headers, SPA same-origin credentials, and no browser storage lint. Verified: npm run check --prefix web; cargo fmt --all -- --check; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace; cargo deny check; cargo deny --features oraclemcp/dashboard-bundle,oraclemcp-core/dashboard-bundle check; scripts/sensitive_data_lint.sh; scripts/oraclemcp_api_lock.sh; scripts/oraclemcp_boundary_lint.sh; scripts/oraclemcp_driver_seam_lint.sh; scripts/oraclemcp_honesty_grep.sh; scripts/release_preflight.sh; dashboard-bundle feature test/clippy.

title: Tests B.8 — WP-W dashboard acceptance suite
description: Appendix B.8. embedded_bundle_served_and_audited (U/Web | new dashboard_e2e); malicious_page_cannot_trigger_gated_action (E/Web | e2e_http_oauth + dashboard_e2e; CSRF/Origin/cookie/clickjacking, no token in localStorage); config_draft_apply_atomic_rollback (E | dashboard_e2e + file_store); workbench_no_bypass_guard_is_the_feature (C/MR/E | trust_safety + dashboard_e2e; same classifier path; equivalence MR workbench-classify == agent-classify; DDL only with dashboard_ddl_workbench; no raw PTY); cp_apply_reclassifies_never_trusts_stored_verdict (E | dashboard_e2e; SEC-1); skin_conformance_2d_fallback_a11y (Web/C | dashboard_e2e; grammar-is-contract, 2D/no-WebGL fallback, a11y, Orrery lazy/code-split budget, secret never rendered). DoD: all green (W8 gated behind dashboard_workbench until no-bypass passes). Depends W1,W2,W8,WDSKIN,WDPR.
close_reason: Implemented B.8 dashboard acceptance accounting and conformance proof map; gates passed.

title: WD-Diff — schema diff + migration export (0.6.2)
description: Schema diff + migration export (section 4-WD 5b, confirmed IN; ships in the 0.6.2 train). Compute a redacted diff between two schema snapshots (or a Change Proposal vs current) and EXPORT a migration script (DDL deploy order, source-replaceable objects first; honest atomicity per WD-RULE-1). Read-classified + gated; the export is a reviewable FILE artifact, not an auto-apply. DoD: schema diff renders; migration export produces a reviewable script; any apply goes through the W8/WDPR gate (SEC-1 re-classify). Depends W7 (schema explorer).
close_reason: Implemented redacted schema diff preview, review-only migration export, and Change Proposal draft path; validated fmt, clippy, workspace tests, cargo deny, API lock, dashboard check, sensitive/boundary/driver/honesty/agent lints, and e2e dry-runs.

title: DEFERRED — ftui TUI front-end (attaches over WP-P)
description: DEFERRED idea (do not start without an operator trigger; N11 no TUI in 0.6.0). An ftui (FrankenTUI, asupersync-native, NOT ratatui) TUI front-end that ATTACHES to the running service over the same versioned operator protocol (WP-P) — the one-core-many-frontends model the web dashboard proves. License = the OpenAI/Anthropic rider already deny-allowed. Trigger: a real demand for a terminal control plane. Depends P1 (the protocol it would consume).

title: W-Sessions — Mission Control home (live sessions; the PRIMARY view)
description: The Mission-Control HOME view (section 4-WD.1 R1 — the single most important view; 'at first glance you see live sessions'). Lists live lanes/sessions: which agent (verified Subject) · which profile/DB · operating level · current activity, with a summary band + expand-to-detail. SSE-driven real-time (section 4-WD.6); the Ground-Control identity + signatures (GO/NO-GO, Clearance Ladder, Countdown) render here; the hero surface (2D board in 0.6.0, Orrery 3D skin in 0.6.2) lives on this view via the WD-Skin BigBoardRenderer seam. Read-only in 0.6.0. Why: this was missing as a distinct bead — W3 is Overview (metrics/charts); Sessions is the home. DoD: live-sessions home renders from SSE, expand-detail, signatures; covered by TB-VIEWS. Depends W1, FN3 (telemetry), FN1 (SSE), WD-Identity.
close_reason: Implemented the Sessions Mission Control home over existing active-lanes, metrics, SSE, capabilities, and connection-info surfaces with summary band, board, signatures, expanded lane detail, event log, and retained per-lane level controls.

title: W-Capacity — capacity view (effective caps, operator reserve, AtCapacity)
description: The Capacity view (one of the 8 IA views; D6/N9-D6). Shows per-profile read-pool + stateful-lane usage vs the EFFECTIVE caps = min(configured 16/8/64, DB per-user/session limit, fd limit, memory budget) computed by startup/doctor (N4); the operator/doctor reserved lanes; AtCapacity events + retry_after; idle reaping. DB-native bits via monitor_profile/self-lane (D5), degrade to monitoring_unavailable when no privilege. Read-only. Why: missing as a distinct bead; the effective-caps surface (N9-D6) had no view. DoD: capacity view renders effective-vs-configured caps + reserve + AtCapacity; covered by TB-VIEWS. Depends W1, N4, FN3.
close_reason: Implemented capacity operator data and dashboard view for effective read-pool/stateful-lane caps, reserve, AtCapacity, retry_after, idle reaping, and monitoring_unavailable limit sources.

title: Tests — dashboard per-view acceptance (section 4-WD.5, the 7+ views)
description: Per-view functional acceptance for the dashboard views NOT covered by the security-headline suite TB B.8 (section 4-WD.5 per-view specs: purpose · panels · real-time-vs-request · operator actions · source; + cross-view shared components). Covers: W-Sessions (home/live-sessions, SSE updates, expand-detail, signatures), W-Capacity (effective-vs-configured caps, reserve, AtCapacity), W3 Overview (metrics/charts), W4 Health (degrades to monitoring_unavailable), W5 Audit (chain verify + filter; operator-sees-all vs scoped-sees-own — D5/OPAUTH), W6 Level (per-lane elevate/drop/TTL), W7 Schema explorer (cache invalidation on DDL/switch), W8b proof bundle, W9 read-only mirror, W10 client-creds (revoke one without others; no secret rendered), WD-IDE (go-to-def/find-usages/lint), WD-Search (global search), WD-History (version-history + source-snapshot revert), shipped BigBoard/skin fallback coverage from WD-Identity + WD-Skin, and WD-Diff (schema diff + migration export). 3D Orrery is intentionally deferred for 0.6.x and must not block this acceptance bead. Each test asserts the §4-WD.5 contract for its view + uses TEST-HARNESS logging. DoD: every shipped view has functional coverage. Depends the shipped view beads + TEST-HARNESS.
close_reason: Dashboard per-view acceptance is covered by dashboard_e2e plus conformance/e2e accounting; full workspace gates and repo lints passed.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Graph repair: removed dependency on deferred WD-Orrery because operator deferred the 3D Orrery for 0.6.x. Per-view acceptance now covers shipped BigBoard/skin fallback plus existing dashboard views, not the deferred optional 3D skin.

title: Tests — 0.6.0 read-only dashboard acceptance gate
description: Codex round-4 finding 2: D1 + §7 put the READ-ONLY CONTROL PLANE in 0.6.0 (Overview/Sessions/Audit/Doctor/Capacity, 2D + skinnable seam, browser-safe auth), but H2 depended on NO WP-W test, so 0.6.0 could tag with no dashboard. This is the 0.6.0 dashboard release gate (depended-on by H2). Covers the read-only views + their §4-WD.5 contracts + browser-safe auth (W1: CSRF/Origin/cookie/no-localStorage) + skinnable 2D/no-WebGL fallback + a11y, with TEST-HARNESS JSON-line logging. The Workbench/PR-board/Orrery (0.6.1/0.6.2) stay gated by their own train test beads, NOT this one. DoD: the 0.6.0 read-only dashboard is fully tested + green before tag. Depends W0,W1,W3,W4,W5,W6,W-Sessions,W-Capacity,WD-Identity,WD-Skin,om-doctor,TEST-HARNESS.
close_reason: Added 0.6.0 read-only dashboard acceptance E2E gate with structured logging, dashboard source-contract test, harness inventory/docs, and green full gates.

title: W2 — profiles + config draft/apply workflow
description: The dashboard NEVER edits the live file in place. Stage a DRAFT → validate with the real strict config loader + redaction tests → optional doctor --profile → REDACTED diff → atomic-rename write with timestamped backup → ask the service to reload (S5) → rollback restores+revalidates. Uses the SCFG backend (shared with setup --write). Sanitized refs (env:/file:/keyring:); NO literal secrets on protected profiles; NO secrets shown/stored (D18). DoD: B.8 config_draft_apply_atomic_rollback. Depends W1, SCFG (cross).
close_reason: Implemented dashboard config draft/apply workflow over existing ConfigOpsBackend with redacted preview, atomic apply, rollback, and S5 profile-drain reload hook.

title: W3 — Overview dashboard (metrics/latency/error/blocked + active-lanes gauge + event log)
description: Tool/latency/error/blocked metrics + active-lanes gauge + a live event log via SSE. Consumes the per-lane telemetry (FN3) + streaming SSE (FN1) + the SSE+watch/broadcast real-time model (section 4-WD.6: SSE invalidates the TanStack Query cache; Last-Event-ID resume; client-side timers). Metrics = a live in-memory ring + append-only per-day history files (FORK-2/D14; OTLP export optional). DoD: dashboards render live; B.8. Depends W1, FN3 (cross), FN1 (cross).
close_reason: Implemented the Overview dashboard with operator metrics, lane gauges, blocked/error/latency summaries, and a live SSE event log consuming per-lane telemetry.

title: W4 — connection health panel
description: pool/latency/role/open-mode/standby/write-posture; DB-native status via the lane self-check or a least-privilege monitor_profile (D5). A missing privilege shows a visible DEGRADED state (monitoring_unavailable), never an error loop or a privilege escalation. Routed through the N-S3 read-classified gate. DoD: health renders + degrades gracefully; B.8. Depends W1, NS3 (cross).
close_reason: Implemented Health dashboard panel using operator health, metrics, and redacted oracle_connection_info with monitoring_unavailable degradation.

title: W5 — audit timeline (+ DB evidence, proof-bundle export)
description: Audit timeline (hash-chain + verify); filter by subject/level/tool; DB-evidence columns + proof-bundle export when available. REDACTION (I3, reconciled with N-S6): the operator (review authority, D17) sees the full SQL TEXT; BIND VALUES are REDACTED BY DEFAULT and require an explicit, AUDITED operator REVEAL (no operator-sees-everything bypass; that old FORK-1 wording is superseded). Scoped non-operators see only their own actions (D5). The signed chain uses sql_sha256, never inlined bind literals (N-S6). DoD: timeline + verify + filtered export; binds redacted by default with audited reveal; B.8. Depends W1, FN2 (audit), N-S6 (redaction newtypes), D17 (operator authority).
close_reason: Implemented audit-tail filters, structural hash-chain proof, DB-evidence columns, and redacted proof-bundle export on /operator/v1/audit-tail; dashboard Audit view now renders filtered timeline/proof export. Timeline/proof exports are allow-list-only: subject hashes only, sql_sha256 only, no SQL preview/text, no bind values; keyed MAC verification remains the offline audit verify path with signing keys. Verified with npm run check --prefix web; cargo fmt --all -- --check; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace; cargo deny check; sensitive_data_lint; api_lock; boundary_lint; driver_seam_lint; honesty_grep; release_preflight.

title: W6 — operating-level control (per-lane elevate/drop)
description: Per-lane preview then elevate then drop, with TTL + confirm — through oracle_set_session_level on the calling lane only (N2). UI shows the target level, TTL, gate decision, and confirm token. DoD: elevate/drop within the profile ceiling; B.8. Depends W1, N2 (cross).
close_reason: Implemented dashboard per-lane operating-level preview/apply/drop over /operator/v1/session/set-level with lane-bound route coverage and frontend type/build verification.

title: W7 — schema/object explorer + bounded metadata cache
description: Schema/object explorer + a bounded metadata cache keyed by DB fingerprint + profile/user + visible schema + serialization-contract version (C5); TTL + byte caps; DDL/commit invalidates affected objects; profile switch invalidates the lane view; NEVER caches result rows/secrets; a hidden profile metadata is never shared with an exposed one. DoD: explorer + cache with correct invalidation; B.8. Depends W1, C5 (cross).
close_reason: Implemented dashboard schema/object explorer with bounded metadata cache and operator metadata routing

title: W8 — Safe SQL Workbench (governed IDE; guard IS the feature)
description: A governed PL/SQL IDE (SQL-Navigator-class) for the human-in-the-loop — NOT a terminal. KEY THESIS: NO fail-closed exception — the human is just another authenticated Subject on its own lane; the guard becomes the feature (preview/diff/audit/snapshot-undo make it safer AND more powerful than SQL Navigator). Four explicit modes: classify_only, read_query, dml_preview_confirm, ddl_plan_confirm — ALL through the same classifier then per-lane ceiling then preview/confirm then rollback/commit then audit path agents use. Read-only default. DDL DISABLED unless the profile sets dashboard_ddl_workbench=true with a matching ceiling; never on protected. WD-RULE-1: DML = Applied (uncommitted; Rollback/TTL-auto-revert; does NOT undo autonomous txns/sequence.NEXTVAL/side-effecting triggers/UTL_*) vs DDL = Applied (committed; Revert re-applies the prior source snapshot ONLY for source-replaceable objects — PL/SQL + views; destructive DDL has no automatic undo). UI shows required level, ceiling, DB fingerprint, lane id, audit subject, row/byte caps, preview impact, and why-blocked + safe alternatives. NO raw PTY/SQLcl shell/arbitrary PL/SQL (N9). RELEASE-GATED behind dashboard_workbench until no-bypass + audit/idempotency tests pass — else the dashboard ships WITHOUT it, unadvertised. DoD: B.8 workbench_no_bypass_guard_is_the_feature (conformance + equivalence MR: workbench-classify equals agent-classify for identical SQL). Depends W1, P1 (cross), WP-B (cross), WP-C (cross).
close_reason: Implemented the browser Workbench over the existing governed MCP path: classify/preview forwards to oracle_preview_sql, read mode forwards to oracle_query, DML rollback/commit forwards to oracle_execute with dashboard CSRF/action tickets and idempotency, and browser DDL/Admin apply is release-gated before dispatch while preview remains available. Added B.8 boundary tests workbench_no_bypass_guard_is_the_feature and dashboard_workbench_ddl_apply_is_release_gated; the UI surfaces required level/danger/lane/subject/sql/audit facts and raw redacted operator envelopes without browser storage. Verified: npm run check --prefix web; cargo fmt --all -- --check; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace; cargo deny check; scripts/sensitive_data_lint.sh; scripts/oraclemcp_api_lock.sh; scripts/oraclemcp_boundary_lint.sh; scripts/oraclemcp_driver_seam_lint.sh; scripts/oraclemcp_honesty_grep.sh; scripts/release_preflight.sh; git diff --check.

title: WP-E — Installer (broad, explicit-consent)
description: WP-E sub-epic. install.sh/install.ps1 (prebuilt musl, SHA256-terminal + cosign verify-blob +verify-attestation, completions, service install ONLY with --service/consent + /readyz gate, dry-run prints every file/unit) + npx + binstall + brew + winget + Docker + MCP registry. Per-client SCOPED creds (unique id + scoped bearer per client, NOT shared; hashed-at-rest, shown-once; revoke/rotate one without others). E0 web-build CI feeds all targets. NO service/client mutation without explicit flag; npx no postinstall side effects. Tests = Appendix B.9. Secrets external refs (D18).
close_reason: Completed the WP-E installer/distribution scope: web-build fan-out, seven-target release artifacts, verified Unix/Windows installers, offline/uninstall/idempotency, scoped client credentials, setup --write, npm/binstall/Homebrew/winget metadata, installer CI smoke, and merged Rust+dashboard release SBOM coverage are all closed in child beads.

title: E0 — web-build CI job (npm ci + vite build → dist; feeds all targets)
description: A web-build CI job (npm ci + vite build → a dist artifact) feeding checks, pinned-nightly, all 6 build targets, and Docker (build JS once, fan to all). Pull rust-embed default-features-off (no web-framework feature → no boundary-lint trip); add it to deny.toml. Pin Node + npm ci for reproducible attestation. Tie to D13 dashboard-bundle (product artifacts ship the bundle; cargo install stays Node-free). DoD: dist built once + embedded; preflight checks embedded-dist-present + content-hash, package-lock.json, no git:/file: JS deps, npm audit high/critical, .crate size. Feeds W0 + H2.
close_reason: Implemented E0 web-build and embedded bundle pipeline: added pinned Node/npm-lock Vite build inputs, rust-embed default-features-off dashboard-bundle feature, native HTTP dashboard fallback outside /operator/v1, release/preflight checks for package-lock/no git-or-file JS deps/npm audit/dist hash/.crate size, CI/release/Docker artifact fan-out, and product builds with the embedded bundle while default cargo-install remains Node-free. Verified npm run check, dashboard bundle validator, cargo fmt --all -- --check, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings, feature clippy for oraclemcp-core/oraclemcp, cargo test --workspace --quiet, feature dashboard test, doc tests/doc build, release_preflight, boundary lint, sensitive-data lint, API lock, cargo deny default and dashboard-feature graphs, cargo package --allow-dirty + crate-size check, and git diff --check.

title: E8 — host the one-liner + README install-first
description: Host the curl-pipe-bash one-liner (and irm|iex for Windows) + make README install-first. DoD: one-liner works on a clean machine; README leads with install + service + dashboard. Depends E2.
close_reason: Pinned the hosted curl one-liner and install-first README ordering with installer e2e coverage; existing installer smoke continues to exercise dry-run, service consent, and no client/service mutation without flags.

title: Tests B.9 + B.13#6 — WP-E installer acceptance suite
description: Appendix B.9 + B.13#6. installer_lint_and_offline_smoke (E | new installer_smoke + CI; shellcheck/PSSA, install built artifact via --offline, NO service/client mutation without --service/consent, dry-run prints every file/unit); per_client_scoped_creds_isolated (E | installer_smoke + e2e_http_oauth; unique id + scoped bearer, revoke one without others, no secret after creation); cosign_and_provenance_verify (E | installer_smoke; SHA256 + cosign verify-blob + verify-attestation). B.13#6 edges: install idempotency (re-install = no-op-or-upgrade, never duplicate units); uninstall reverses all; --offline missing-bundle → typed error; Rosetta/musl detect. DoD: all green. Depends E2,E4,E7.
close_reason: Completed WP-E installer acceptance: built-artifact smoke now verifies idempotent offline reinstall, installer keeps different-content replacement gated by --force, and E2E docs/tests pin the B.9/B.13 contract

title: S-sbom — JS CycloneDX SBOM merged into the release SBOM + attest subjects
description: section 4-R S-sbom. Generate a CycloneDX SBOM from the SPA lockfile and MERGE it into the release SBOM (today the SBOM is Rust-only so the supply-chain claim is false for the embedded JS). Add the merged SBOM as a cosign/attestation subject. DoD: release SBOM covers Rust + JS; attestation subjects include it; preflight checks the SBOM is present + current. Depends E0 (web-build produces the lockfile/dist).
close_reason: Merged Rust and dashboard CycloneDX SBOMs into the signed release SBOM, added source/artifact preflight checks, and covered the release workflow with tests.

title: E1 — release matrix + aarch64-musl (static-verify musl)
description: Build matrix incl. aarch64-musl (cross toolchain; static-verify musl only). Per-target binary-size budget. DoD: all 7 targets build + are size-checked. Blocks E2/E3 (the installer downloads these) + F1/F2/F3.
close_reason: Expanded release.yml to a seven-target binary matrix including aarch64-unknown-linux-musl via cargo-zigbuild, added per-target binary-size budgets, and static ELF checks for musl artifacts; documented the archive matrix.

title: E2 — install.sh (prebuilt, verified, explicit-consent service)
description: install.sh: prebuilt musl, SHA256-terminal + cosign verify-blob AND verify-attestation, completions, service install ONLY with --service or interactive consent + a /readyz health-gate, Rosetta detect, source opt-in, and a dry-run that prints EVERY file/unit it would touch. SHA256 = transport integrity, NOT authenticity (cosign is authenticity — say so in docs). Installs the om alias (A8). DoD: B.9 installer_lint_and_offline_smoke (shellcheck) + cosign_and_provenance_verify; no service mutation without the flag. Depends E1, A0 (completions, cross), A8 (om, cross), S1 (service, cross).
close_reason: Implemented verified install.sh with prebuilt archive checksum/cosign/blob-attestation verification, om/completions install plan, explicit service consent, offline smoke coverage, and docs.

title: E3 — install.ps1 (certutil checksum, consent)
description: install.ps1: certutil checksum verification, completions, service install only with consent, dry-run. PSSA-clean. DoD: B.9 (PSSA + smoke). Depends E1.
close_reason: Implemented install.ps1 for the Windows release zip: certutil SHA-256 verification, cosign blob/signature/provenance verification, offline bundle checks, PowerShell completions, dry-run file/unit/service plan, and service install gated by -Service -Yes. README and installer_e2e pin the Windows install/offline/service contract; installer smoke now checks install.ps1 statically and runs PowerShell/PSScriptAnalyzer/dry-runs when pwsh is available. Verified locally with containerized PSScriptAnalyzer + PowerShell dry-runs, installer smoke, installer_e2e, fmt, clippy, workspace tests, cargo deny, and repo lints.

title: E4 — opt-in MCP-client auto-registration (scoped per-client creds)
description: Opt-in MCP-client auto-registration: a UNIQUE client id + a SCOPED bearer per client (claude mcp add --transport http, verified paths, stdio-only-never-allow-no-auth, no secrets in configs, revocation metadata, a rotation command). NOT a shared bearer (a shared bearer destroys audit). DoD: B.9 per_client_scoped_creds_isolated (revoke/rotate one without others; no secret after creation). Depends E2, E-cred, A8 (om, cross).
close_reason: Implemented opt-in per-client HTTP credential plumbing: serve --client-credentials, clients issue/list/rotate/revoke, service and installer registration hooks, HTTP scoped-principal auth, docs, and isolation/redaction tests. Verified with fmt, clippy, workspace tests, deny, sensitive-data lint, boundary lint, installer smoke, and release preflight.

title: E-cred — per-client credential store (service-owned, hashed, atomic)
description: The per-client credential store at the XDG state dir clients.json (0600, dir 0700, Windows ACL), atomic-rename + advisory lock, SERVICE OWNS WRITES (the installer calls a client add the service applies). Creds hashed-at-rest, shown-once (FORK-3). revoke/rotate → immediate lane teardown (N5) + grant revocation; in the S5 reload set; list/revoke/rotate operator routes enumerated in P1. DoD: store with atomic writes + hashing + lifecycle; feeds E4/W10. Depends S5 (cross), SFILE (cross).
close_reason: Added service-owned clients.json credential store with salted bearer hashes, one-time issuance, verify/list/rotate/revoke lifecycle, and principal-level lane teardown hook for grant revocation

title: E5 — setup --write (shares the SCFG backend)
description: setup --write shares the SCFG config-ops backend (draft→validate→atomic-rename+backup→reload). Generates generic local setup templates (profiles, wrappers, MCP-client snippets). DoD: setup --write round-trips via SCFG; B.9. Depends E2, SCFG (cross).
close_reason: Implemented setup --write via SCFG config-ops with redacted output and binary round-trip test

title: E6 — uninstall + --offline
description: Uninstall reverses ALL touched files/units (idempotent; never duplicates on re-install). --offline mode for air-gapped installs (typed error if a needed bundle is missing — never a silent partial install). DoD: uninstall reverses everything; B.13 part 6 installer idempotency. Depends E2.
close_reason: Implemented explicit uninstall and offline archive modes in install.sh: offline installs verify a local archive plus required checksum/signature/cert/attestation siblings before extraction, uninstall is preview/--yes gated and idempotently removes installer-owned files with service removal still requiring --service, and installer smoke/e2e/README contracts cover the new paths. Verified with installer smoke, installer e2e, fmt, clippy, workspace tests, cargo deny, and repo lints.

title: E7 — installer CI lint + smoke (built artifact, no mutation without flag)
description: CI lint (shellcheck/PSSA) + smoke: install the BUILT artifact via --offline; assert NO service/client mutation without the flag; dry-run prints every file/unit. DoD: B.9 green in CI. Depends E2, E3.
close_reason: Wired installer CI gates: Ubuntu builds the dashboard-bundled musl binary and runs install.sh --offline through the installer smoke with shellcheck required and no service/client flags, while Windows runs PowerShell parse, PSScriptAnalyzer, and dry-run service/no-service assertions for install.ps1. Release acceptance now asserts the installer gates and schedules installer_lint_and_offline_smoke; installer_e2e/e2e_harness pin the CI contract. Verified with built-artifact offline smoke, release acceptance dry-run, workflow YAML parse, installer_e2e, e2e_harness, fmt, clippy, workspace tests, cargo deny, and repo lints.

title: Epic: Truthful MCP tool annotations + outputSchema
description:
Idea #4. structuredContent is ALREADY emitted (server.rs:516); the gaps are (a) MCP tool annotations and (b) declared outputSchema. ToolSpec/ToolDescriptor carry only input_schema + an internal `destructive` bool (tools.rs); the wire emits only name/description/inputSchema.
CRITICAL: MCP annotation spec DEFAULTS ARE UNSAFE -- omitted destructiveHint=true, openWorldHint=true, readOnlyHint=false, idempotentHint=false. A fail-closed read-only server MUST set all four explicitly on read tools, or an omitted annotation reads as "destructive, open-world." genai-toolbox actually ships Oracle tools advertised destructive while running read-only; we can be the server that gets this right.
Annotations stay advisory (clients MUST treat them as untrusted) -- the server-side classifier remains the real gate.
Source: res-protocol #2/#3, res-pgpro #10, res-oracle (a)#7.
close_reason: All annotation/outputSchema children are closed: tools/list emits explicit titles and all four MCP annotations, oracle_query/query/oracle_explain_plan declare outputSchema with NUMBER-as-string preserved, and conformance/e2e/golden/docs coverage is green.

title: Add MCP tool annotations to ToolSpec and emit them on the wire

Set EXPLICIT values on every tool: read tools (oracle_query/explain_plan/describe*/get_*/search_*/list_*/sample_rows/capabilities) -> readOnlyHint=true,destructiveHint=false,idempotentHint=true,openWorldHint=false; the inert write/DDL tools -> readOnlyHint=false,destructiveHint=true.

title: Epic: Read-only DBA diagnostic tool suite
description:
Idea #2/#5/#14 -- the Postgres-MCP-Pro-style differentiator, a perfect fit for a read-only server (pure V$/DBA_* reads + Oracle's own advisor engines).
VERIFIED: oraclemcp-db/src/awr.rs already has top_sql_query + DiagnosticsSource{AwrAsh,Statspack} + detect_statspack + select_diagnostics_source (license-gated, AWR DBA_HIST_* needs Diagnostics Pack, falls back to Statspack STATS$*). NO MCP tool surfaces it yet -- so oracle_top_queries SURFACES existing code, it does not rebuild it.
Health subchecks map to Oracle: invalid objects (DBA_OBJECTS), unusable/unused indexes (DBA_INDEXES,V$OBJECT_USAGE), tablespace+UNDO headroom (DBA_TABLESPACE_USAGE_METRICS), sequence ceiling (DBA_SEQUENCES), disabled/NOVALIDATE constraints (DBA_CONSTRAINTS), buffer cache hit (V$BUFFER_POOL_STATISTICS/V$SYSSTAT). Advisor = DBMS_ADVISOR/DBMS_SQLTUNE (recommendations only, read-compatible; Tuning Pack gated).
Source: res-pgpro #1/#2/#3/#4/#7. All packs must be license-gated like the existing AWR path and preflighted in doctor.
notes: v0.3.0 release decision: defer the read-only DBA diagnostic suite to a post-RC feature wave. It needs careful privilege degradation, Diagnostics/Tuning Pack gating, doctor preflight UX, SQL-shape tests, and live Oracle coverage; shipping it late would expand the surface area beyond the stable thin-native release candidate.
close_reason: superseded by the 0.4.0 plan bead conversion (oraclemcp-040-* epics)
